Don’t think you’re wise in your own eyes; honor the Lord and steer clear of sin. Doing this will lead to good health and renewed strength. Honor the Lord with your wealth and give Him the best of what you receive, and your storage will be filled with grain while your jars overflow with wine. A greedy person creates conflict, but those who trust the Lord will thrive. Commit everything you do to the Lord, and your plans will be successful.

title: The Way To Be Well And Happy
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk: Many people often talk about their own kindness. But where can we find someone who is truly trustworthy? You can tell a lot about a child by their actions, whether what they do is pure and right. When worry fills a person's mind, it brings sadness, but a kind word can bring happiness. A person's spirit helps them through sickness, but who can lift a broken spirit? A calm mind gives life to the body, while jealousy only causes decay. A joyful heart creates a cheerful face, but inward sorrow can break a spirit. A joyful heart is like good medicine, whereas a broken spirit can dry up a person’s essence.

title: The Importance Of Being Willing To Learn
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk: Wise people share their knowledge, but fools often can't see it. A fool believes they always know best, while a wise person is open to advice. Fools ignore their father's guidance, but those who accept correction show their wisdom. It's important to listen to advice and be open to learning; this will help you be wise as you grow older. Advice can be like a deep well, and a sensible person knows how to bring it out. Cling to what you learn; it’s essential for your life. My child, don’t disregard the lessons from the Lord, and don’t push away guidance, because those He cares for, He also corrects, just like a loving father does with his son. A person who refuses to accept correction is hurting themselves, but one who listens to advice grows in understanding.

title: How To Honor Your Parents
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk: Son, pay attention to your father's guidance and don't ignore your mother's teachings. They’ll be like a beautiful crown on your head and a lovely necklace around your neck. Listen to your dad, who brought you into this world, and don’t take your mother for granted as she gets older. A wise son follows instructions. But if you choose to hang out with spendthrifts, you’ll bring shame to your dad. If you steal from your parents, thinking there’s nothing wrong with it, you’re just like someone who destroys things. If you curse your parents, your light will fade into the darkest night. A wise son brings joy to his father, while a foolish son shows disrespect to his mother. A foolish son is a source of sorrow to his dad and causes pain to the one who gave him life. So, be wise, my son, and make my heart happy so I can answer those who criticize me. Let your father be filled with joy and let your mother celebrate.

title: The Troubles Of Those Who Are Lazy
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk: The lazy guy says, "There's a lion outside! I’m going to get killed in the streets!" As the door swings on its hinges, the lazy guy rolls over in bed. He dips his hand into the dish but doesn’t bother to bring it to his mouth! In his own mind, the lazy man thinks he’s smarter than seven people who can think critically. The lazy person's path is full of thorns, while the diligent one's road is like a well-paved highway. I walked past the lazy man's field, the vineyard of the person who just didn’t have common sense. It was completely overgrown with thorns, covered in nettles, and the stone wall was all broken down. Then I paused and thought about it; I realized something important: A little extra sleep, a little more dozing off, a little folding of the hands for rest, and suddenly your poverty will sneak up on you like a thief, and your needs will come at you like a sturdy soldier.

title: Things That Are Better Than Riches
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk: A person might be poor but live with integrity, and that's better than someone who's wealthy but dishonest. Having a little while doing the right thing is far better than having a lot through unfair means. It's more valuable to have a humble spirit among the modest than to share in the spoils with the arrogant. A good reputation is worth more than great wealth and is prized even more than silver and gold. Those who rely on riches will ultimately fail, but those who do what's right will thrive like a green leaf. Don't work just to get rich; don't lean solely on your own understanding. If you set your sights on wealth, it will vanish! Riches disappear quickly, like an eagle soaring into the sky. It's better to have a little while respecting the Lord than to have a lot along with troubles. There are two things I ask of You, God. Please don't deny me these before I die: keep deceit and lies far from me, and don't let me experience either poverty or riches. Just give me the food I need so I won’t be too full and forget about You, saying, 'Who is the Lord?' Or worse, become poor and steal, bringing shame to Your name.

title: Right Eating And Drinking
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk: If you come across honey, only take what you need so you don't eat too much and end up feeling sick. Wine can lead to trouble, and strong drinks can make you fight with others. Anyone who gets misled by them isn’t wise. Who cries out in despair? Who complains? Who has unnecessary battles and wounds? Who has red eyes? It’s those who spend too much time drinking wine, those who go for mixed drinks. So don't be fooled by wine when it’s red, when it sparkles in the glass, and slides down smoothly. In the end, it bites like a snake and stings like a poisonous snake. Then you’ll see strange things, and your mind will be all foggy. You’ll feel like someone lost at sea, like someone sleeping through a wild storm. "I’ve been hit, but I don’t feel any pain. I’ve been hurt, but I don’t even notice. When will I wake up from all this drinking? I’ll go back for more."

title: The Right Use Of The Tongue
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk: Have you ever noticed someone who talks before thinking? There’s more hope for a fool than for that person. Anyone who responds before truly listening just ends up embarrassing themselves. But those who are mindful with their words show they’re wise, and someone with a calm attitude is particularly sensible. Even a fool seems wise when they choose to stay quiet; they appear careful when they keep their mouth shut. A person who watches what they say can save themselves from trouble. The power of life and death lies in what we say; those who love to talk will face the consequences of their words. A person finds joy in speaking, and the right words at the right time are truly wonderful! When words are spoken well, it’s like having golden fruit in a silver dish. Sweet words are like honeycomb—enjoyable to taste and restorative for the soul.

title: The Control Of The Temper
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk: A gentle response can soothe anger, but a sharp word can fuel it. A person who lacks self-control is like a city with broken walls—vulnerable and easy to conquer. An impulsive person gives in to their rage, while a wise individual knows how to keep their cool. A fool's anger is visible right away, but a sensible person doesn't let insults get to them. Patience shows a man's wisdom; it's an honor to overlook the faults of others. Someone who can stay calm is stronger than a warrior, and controlling one’s temper is mightier than capturing a city.

title: What It Means To Be Modest
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk: Don't brag about what you plan to do tomorrow because you have no idea what tomorrow will bring. Have you noticed someone who’s full of themselves? There’s more hope for a fool than for someone so proud! Pride leads to ruin, and an arrogant attitude brings a downfall. When pride steps in, shame follows close behind, but true wisdom belongs to the humble. It’s not good to overindulge in sweet things, but seeking the success of others is truly admirable. Let others sing your praises instead of doing it yourself; let someone else acknowledge your achievements.

title: Being Generous And Loving
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk: If someone returns bad deeds for good ones, trouble will follow him wherever he goes. It takes real strength for a person to avoid conflicts; only a fool starts arguments. Just like charcoal fuels a fire, a quarrelsome person sparks strife! Don’t pick fights without a reason—if someone hasn’t hurt you, leave things be. Starting conflicts is like letting water flow out; it’s better to prevent them before they escalate. It’s far better to have a simple meal in peace than a lavish feast filled with fighting. A good person cares for his animals, while the wicked show no mercy. Those who take advantage of the poor insult their Maker, but those who show kindness to those in need honor Him. If you ignore the cries of the poor, your own pleas will go unanswered. When you help the poor, it’s like lending to the Lord, and He will reward you for your kindness. If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he’s thirsty, give him a drink. By doing so, you’ll make him feel the weight of your kindness, and the Lord will bless you. Hatred only leads to conflict, but love can overlook mistakes. A simple meal shared with love is better than a feast of rich foods filled with hate. Keep love and truth close to you; let them guide you. This way, you’ll find favor and a good name in the eyes of both God and people.

title: Jesus Is Born At Bethlehem
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
The angel Gabriel was sent by God to a young woman named Mary in Nazareth, a town in Galilee. She was engaged to a man named Joseph, who came from the family of David. When the angel approached her, he said, "Greetings, favored one! God is with you!"

Mary was taken aback by his words and wondered what such a greeting could mean. But the angel reassured her, saying, "Don’t be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God. You will conceive a son, and you will name him Jesus. He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High."

Mary replied to the angel, "How can this happen? I’m not married yet." The angel responded, "The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you; therefore, your child will be called holy, the Son of God." Mary then said, "I am God's servant. Let it be as you have said." After that, the angel left her.

During that time, Emperor Augustus ordered that everyone should be registered. So, everyone went to their hometowns to register. Because Joseph was from the family of David, he traveled with Mary, his wife, from Nazareth in Galilee to Bethlehem in Judea, the birthplace of David. While they were there, Mary gave birth to her first son. She wrapped him in swaddling clothes and laid him in a manger, because there was no room for them at the inn.

In that region, there were shepherds living in the fields, keeping watch over their flocks at night. An angel from God appeared to them, and a bright light shone all around them, which frightened them. But the angel said to them,

"Don’t be afraid, for I bring you good news of great joy that will be for all the people. Today, in the town of David, a Savior has been born to you; He is God’s Anointed One. This will be a sign for you: you will find a baby wrapped in swaddling clothes, lying in a manger."

Suddenly, a multitude of heavenly beings appeared with the angel, praising God and saying,

"Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, goodwill toward all men."

Once the angels had left and returned to heaven, the shepherds said to one another, "Let’s go to Bethlehem and see this thing that God has made known to us." They hurried off and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby who was lying in a manger.

After they had seen Him, they shared what had been told to them about this child. Everyone who heard their story was amazed, but Mary kept all these things close to her heart and pondered them often. The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen, just as it had been told.

title: The Wise Men Bring Gifts To The Young Child
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
When Jesus was born in Bethlehem, wise men from the east arrived in Jerusalem and asked, "Where is the newborn King of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and came to worship him."

When Herod heard this, he and everyone else in Jerusalem were very disturbed. He gathered all the high priests and scribes together and asked them where the Messiah was supposed to be born. They replied, "In Bethlehem of Judea."

Herod then called the wise men aside and asked them how long ago they had seen the star appear in the east. He sent them to Bethlehem with a command: "Go and search diligently for the young child, and when you find him, let me know, so I can go and worship him too." After they listened to the king, they went on their way, and the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the young child was. They were filled with joy at the sight; and when they entered the house and saw the child with his mother Mary, they knelt down and worshipped him. They opened their treasures and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh. But after being warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they decided to go back to their own country a different way.

title: The Boy Jesus Asks Questions
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
After Joseph and Mary had fulfilled all the requirements of the law, they returned to their hometown of Nazareth in Galilee. The boy Jesus grew up strong in both body and mind, and God's blessing was upon him.

Every year, his parents traveled to Jerusalem for the Feast of the Passover. When Jesus turned twelve, they made the trip as usual. After spending the required days there, they started their journey home, unaware that the boy Jesus had stayed behind in Jerusalem. Assuming he was with the caravan, they traveled for a whole day before realizing he was missing and began looking for him among their relatives and friends. When they couldn’t find him, they returned to Jerusalem, still searching for him. After three days, they found him in the Temple, sitting with the teachers, listening to them, and asking questions. Everyone who heard him was amazed by his intelligence and his responses.

When his parents finally saw him, they were shocked. His mother said, "Son, why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been searching for you anxiously!" Jesus replied, "Why were you searching for me? Didn’t you know I had to be in my Father’s house?" But they didn’t understand what he meant.

Then he went back with them to Nazareth and was obedient to them. His mother treasured all these things in her heart. And Jesus continued to grow in wisdom, strength, and in favor with both God and people.

title: John The Baptist Tells Of Jesus' Work
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
During the time when Pontius Pilate was the governor of Judea and Herod ruled over Galilee, a man named John, the son of Zachariah, was living in the desert. God gave him a mission, and he traveled throughout the area near the Jordan River, urging people to get baptized as a sign of their regret for their sins and their desire for forgiveness. Those who genuinely felt remorse for their wrongdoings were baptized in the Jordan.

John wore clothes made from camel’s hair and a leather belt, and his diet consisted of locusts and wild honey.

Soon, people from Jerusalem, all of Judea, and the surrounding areas began to come to him to admit their sins and to be baptized by him in the Jordan.

To the crowds coming to be baptized, he exclaimed, "You brood of vipers! Who warned you to escape from the coming judgment? Produce fruit that shows you are truly sorry for your actions. Don’t think, ‘We are descendants of Abraham’; because I tell you, God can create descendants for Abraham from these stones. The axe is already laid at the root of the trees; every tree that doesn’t bear good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire."

The crowds kept asking him, "What should we do?" He replied, "If you have two coats, share with those who don’t have any, and if you have food, do the same."

When the tax collectors came to be baptized, they asked him, "Teacher, what should we do?" He answered, "Don’t collect more than what you’re required to." Soldiers also approached him, asking, "And what about us?" He said, "Don’t extort money from anyone or accuse anyone falsely; be satisfied with your pay."

As the people were curious if John might be the Messiah, he spoke to them all, "I indeed baptize you with water; but someone mightier than I is coming, someone whose sandals I’m not worthy to untie. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire. His winnowing fork is in His hand to clear His threshing floor and to gather the wheat into His barn, but He will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire." With many other sincere words, he shared the good news with the people.

title: Jesus Decides How He Will Do His Work
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
At that moment, Jesus arrived from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan River. As he came up from the water, he noticed the heavens opening up and the Spirit, appearing like a dove, descending upon him. Then, a voice echoed from heaven saying,

"You are my beloved Son; I am truly pleased with you."

Next, the Spirit led Jesus into the wilderness to face temptation from the devil. After fasting for forty days and nights, he became hungry. Then the tempter approached him and said, "If you’re really the Son of God, tell these stones to turn into bread." But Jesus replied, "It is written,

"'People do not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from God.'"

The devil then took him to the holy city and placed him at the highest point of the Temple, saying, "If you are the Son of God, jump off; for it is written,

"'He will command his angels concerning you, and they will lift you up in their hands, so you won’t even strike your foot against a stone.'"

Jesus responded, "It is also written,

Once more, the devil led him to a high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory. He said, "All of this I will give to you if you bow down and worship me." Jesus answered, "Go away, Satan! For it is written,

"'You must worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.'"

title: Jesus Wins Devoted Friends
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Herod captured John the Baptist, locked him up in prison because of Herodias, his brother Philip's wife. John had told Herod, "It's not right for you to have her." Even though Herod wanted to kill John, he was afraid of the people because they believed John was a prophet.

After John was thrown in prison, Jesus went to Galilee to share the good news from God: "The time has come; turn away from your sins and believe the good news, for the kingdom of God is near."

Leaving Nazareth, Jesus settled in Capernaum, by the Sea of Galilee. As He walked along the shore, He saw Simon and his brother Andrew casting their nets into the water, because they were fishermen. Jesus called out to them, "Come with me, and I’ll make you fishers of men." Immediately, they dropped their nets and followed Him. A little further down, He saw James, the son of Zebedee, and his brother John, who were in their boat fixing their nets. He called them too, and right away they left their father, Zebedee, in the boat with the hired hands and went with Him.

Next, Jesus found Philip and said, "Come with me." Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter. Then Philip found Nathanael and told him, "We’ve found the one that Moses and the prophets talked about: Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph." Nathanael replied, "Can anything good come out of Nazareth?" Philip answered, "Come and see."

As Jesus saw Nathanael approaching, He said, "Here’s a true Israelite, someone without deceit." Nathanael asked Him, "How do you know me?" Jesus replied, "Before Philip called you, I saw you sitting under the fig tree." Nathanael responded, "Rabbi, You are the Son of God, You are the King of Israel." Jesus said, "You believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You’ll see greater things than these!"

title: The Wedding At Cana
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk: Two days later, a wedding took place in Cana of Galilee, and Jesus' mother was there. Jesus and his disciples had also received invitations. When they ran out of wine, Jesus' mother approached him and said, "They've run out of wine." Jesus replied, "What does that have to do with me? My time hasn’t come yet." His mother turned to the servants and instructed, "Do whatever he tells you." Six large stone jars, typically used by the Jews for ceremonial washing, were standing there, each capable of holding about twenty gallons. Jesus said, "Fill the jars with water." So, they filled them to the top. Then he instructed, "Now draw some out and take it to the person in charge of the feast." They followed his directions. When the head of the feast tasted the water that had turned into wine, he was unaware of its origin (though the servants who had drawn the water knew). He called the bridegroom over and remarked, "Everyone serves the best wine first, and when people have had their fill, then the cheaper wine comes out. But you have saved the best wine until now!" This was the first of the miraculous signs that Jesus performed in Cana of Galilee, and it confirmed his disciples' faith in him.

title: The Healing Of The Ten Lepers
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
As Jesus and his disciples traveled on their way to Jerusalem, they made their way through Samaria and Galilee. When they entered a village, they encountered ten lepers who called out to him from a distance, saying, "Jesus, Master, have mercy on us." When Jesus saw them, he replied, "Go and show yourselves to the priests." And as they walked away, they found that they had been healed.

One of the lepers, realizing he had been healed, turned back and loudly praised God. He fell at Jesus' feet, face down, and thanked him. This man was a Samaritan. Jesus asked him, "Didn’t I heal ten of you? Where are the other nine? Isn’t this foreigner the only one who came back to give praise to God?" Then Jesus told him, "Get up and go; your faith has made you whole."

title: Healing The Man At The Pool
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
During a Jewish festival in Jerusalem, Jesus made his way to the city. Near the sheep gate, there was a pool called Bethesda, surrounded by five covered porches. A crowd of people—sick, blind, lame, and in despair—lay there, waiting for the waters to stir. An angel of the Lord would come down at certain times to move the water, and the first person to jump in after it was stirred would be healed, regardless of their illness.

In the crowd was a man who had been sick for thirty-eight years. Jesus noticed him lying there and realized how long he had been suffering. He approached the man and asked, "Do you want to be healed?" The sick man replied, "Sir, I don't have anyone to help me into the pool when the water is stirred. By the time I try to get in, someone else takes my place." Jesus responded, "Get up, grab your mat, and walk." Instantly, the man was healed, and he picked up his mat and began to walk.

title: Jesus Talks With A Samaritan Woman
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
As Jesus left Judea and headed back to Galilee, he passed through Samaria and came to a city called Sychar, close to the land Jacob had given to his son Joseph. Jacob's well was there, so feeling tired from his journey, Jesus sat down by the well. It was around noon when a Samaritan woman arrived to draw water. Jesus said to her, "Could you give me a drink?" (His disciples had gone into the city to buy food.)

The Samaritan woman replied, "How can you, a Jew, ask me for a drink? I’m a Samaritan!" The Jews generally didn’t interact with Samaritans. Jesus told her, "If you knew the gift of God and who it is saying, 'Give me a drink,' you would have asked him, and he would have given you living water." The woman responded, "Sir, you don't have anything to draw water with, and the well is deep. Where will you get that living water? Are you greater than our ancestor Jacob, who gave us this well and drank from it with his children and livestock?" Jesus said, "Anyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again, but whoever drinks the water I give will never be thirsty. The water I provide will become a spring of water bubbling up to eternal life." The woman said, "Sir, please give me this water so I won’t ever be thirsty again and won't have to keep coming here to draw water."

Jesus told her, "Go call your husband and return here." The woman said, "I don’t have a husband." Jesus replied, "You’re right when you say you have no husband, for you’ve had five husbands, and the man you’re with now isn’t your husband; you’ve spoken the truth."

title: Jesus Talks With A Samaritan Woman
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
The woman said to him, "Sir, I see you’re a prophet. Our ancestors worshipped on this mountain, but you Jews say that Jerusalem is the place to worship." Jesus replied, "Believe me, the time is coming when you won’t worship the Father on this mountain or in Jerusalem. The true worshippers will worship the Father in spirit and truth; these are the worshippers the Father seeks. God is spirit, and those who worship him must do so in spirit and truth." The woman said, "I know that the Messiah (who is called Christ) is coming. When he arrives, he will explain everything to us." Jesus declared, "The one speaking to you is he."

Just then, the disciples returned and were surprised to find him talking to a woman, but none of them asked, "What do you want?" or, "Why are you talking to her?"

The woman left her water jar behind and went back to the city, saying to the men, "Come see a man who told me everything I ever did! Could this be the Messiah?" They headed out from the town to see him.

Meanwhile, the disciples urged him, "Rabbi, eat something." But he replied, "I have food to eat that you know nothing about." They looked at each other and said, "Did someone bring him food?" Jesus explained, "My food is to do the will of him who sent me and to finish his work. Don’t you have a saying, 'It’s still four months until harvest'? I tell you, open your eyes and look at the fields! They are ripe for harvest right now! The reaper is already collecting his wages and gathering a crop for eternal life, so the sower and the reaper can rejoice together. For here is a saying that’s true: 'One sows and another reaps.' I sent you to harvest what you didn't plant; others have done the hard work, and you have reaped the benefits of their labor."

Many Samaritans from that town believed in Jesus because of the woman’s testimony, which said, "He told me everything I ever did." When they came to see him, they urged him to stay, and he stayed for two days. Many more believed because of what he said. They told the woman, "We no longer believe just because of what you said; now we have heard for ourselves and we know this is truly the Savior of the world."

title: Giving Life To A Widow'S Son
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Jesus traveled to a town called Nain, accompanied by his disciples and a large crowd. As they approached the town gate, he noticed a funeral procession. A young man, who was the only son of a widow, was being carried out, and many townsfolk were there to support her.

When Jesus saw the widow, he felt a deep compassion for her and said, "Don’t cry." He stepped up and touched the coffin, causing the bearers to pause. Then he said, "Young man, I tell you, get up!" To everyone’s amazement, the young man sat up and began to talk. Jesus returned him to his mother. Fear spread through the crowd, and they praised God, saying, "A great prophet has risen among us, and God has come to help his people." News of what Jesus had done spread throughout Judea and the surrounding regions.

title: Jesus Heals The Sick
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Jesus and his disciples arrived in Capernaum, and the following Sabbath, he entered the synagogue to teach. The people were amazed by his teaching; he spoke with authority, unlike the scribes.

That day, there was a man in the synagogue possessed by an unclean spirit who shouted, "What do you want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Are you here to destroy us? I know you're God's Holy One!" Jesus firmly said to the unclean spirit, "Be quiet, and come out of him." The spirit threw the man into a fit before leaving him with a loud cry. The crowd was so shocked that they began to ask one another, "What is this? Is this a new teaching? He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him!" News about Jesus spread quickly throughout Galilee.

After leaving the synagogue, they headed straight to the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John joining them. Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with a fever, so they immediately told Jesus about her. He approached her, took her hand, and helped her up. The fever left her, and she began to serve them.

Later that evening, after sunset, people brought all the sick and those troubled by evil spirits to him until everyone from the city was at the door. He healed many who were suffering from various diseases and cast out numerous evil spirits, but he didn’t allow them to speak because they recognized him.

One day, a leper approached him, kneeling and begging, "If you are willing, you can make me clean." Jesus, feeling compassion for him, reached out and touched him, saying, "I am willing; be cleansed!" Immediately, the leprosy left him, and he was healed. Then Jesus strictly instructed him, "Don't tell anyone about this. Go, show yourself to the priest, and offer the sacrifices that Moses commanded as proof to them that you are clean." Despite this, the man went out and began to share the news everywhere, making it difficult for Jesus to enter cities openly; instead, he had to stay in secluded places, and people came to him from all over.

title: Jesus Cures A Man Who Cannot Walk
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
When Jesus arrived back in Capernaum after a few days, everyone heard he was home. So many people crowded around that there wasn’t even room at the door. While Jesus was teaching, four men showed up, carrying a paralyzed man who couldn't move. Since they couldn’t get through the crowd, they climbed up and started tearing apart the roof above Jesus. Once they made a big enough hole, they lowered the bed with the man on it. Seeing their faith, Jesus said to him, "Son, your sins are forgiven." But some scribes in the crowd thought to themselves, "Why is he saying this? It's blasphemy! Only God can forgive sins!"

Jesus immediately knew what they were thinking and said, "Why are you thinking these things? Which is easier: to tell the paralyzed man, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Get up, take your bed, and walk'? But so you know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins—" and he turned to the man who couldn’t walk and said, "I tell you, get up, take your bed, and go home." The man immediately got up, picked up his bed, and walked out in front of everyone. They were all amazed and praised God, saying, "We’ve never seen anything like this!"

title: Jesus Makes Even Wrong-Doers His Friends
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Once again, Jesus went out beside the Sea of Galilee, and a huge crowd gathered around him. He began to teach them. As he walked along, he spotted Levi, the son of Alphaeus, sitting at the tax booth. He called out to him, "Come follow me." Levi instantly got up and followed him.

Later, while Jesus was having dinner at Levi's house, a bunch of tax collectors and other sinners joined him and his disciples. The scribes and Pharisees noticed this and asked his disciples, "Why does he eat with such people?" Jesus overheard them and replied, "It's not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick. I didn't come to call the righteous, but sinners to change their ways."

On another occasion, one of the Pharisees invited Jesus to dinner. So, Jesus went to the Pharisee's house and sat down at the table. In that town, there was a woman known to be sinful. When she learned that Jesus was dining at the Pharisee's house, she brought an alabaster jar filled with perfume. She stood behind him, weeping, and her tears began to fall on his feet. She wiped them with her hair, kissed his feet lovingly, and poured the perfume on them.

When the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw this, he thought to himself, "If this man were really a prophet, he'd know what sort of woman is touching him—she's a sinner!"

Jesus replied to him, "Simon, I have something to say to you." Simon answered, "Go ahead, teacher." Jesus continued, "Two men owed a certain moneylender some money: one owed five hundred silver coins, the other fifty. When neither could pay him back, he forgave them both. Now, which of them will love him more?" Simon responded, "I guess it'll be the one who owed more." Jesus said, "You've judged correctly."

Turning to the woman, Jesus said to Simon, "Do you see this woman? When I came into your house, you didn’t give me any water for my feet, but she has wet my feet with her tears and dried them with her hair. You didn’t greet me with a kiss, but she has been kissing my feet since I arrived. You didn’t anoint my head with oil, but she has poured perfume on my feet. Therefore, I tell you, her many sins have been forgiven because she has shown great love. But someone who has been forgiven little loves little."

Then Jesus said to her, "Your sins are forgiven." The other guests started murmuring among themselves, "Who is this man who even forgives sins?" But Jesus said to the woman, "Your faith has saved you; go in peace."

title: Jesus Finds Joy In All His Work
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
One day, while John's disciples and the Pharisees were observing a fast, some people approached Jesus and asked, "Why do John's disciples and the Pharisees' disciples fast, but your disciples do not?"

Jesus replied, "Can wedding guests fast while the bridegroom is still with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they can't fast. But there will come a time when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast. Nobody sews a patch of new cloth onto an old coat; if they do, the patch will pull away from the coat, worsening the tear. And no one pours new wine into old wine-skins; if they do, the new wine will burst the skins, and both the wine and the skins will be ruined. Instead, new wine must be poured into fresh wine-skins."

On a Sabbath, Jesus was walking through some grain fields, and as his disciples walked along, they began to pick some heads of grain. The Pharisees said to him, "Look, why are they doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath?" Jesus replied, "Have you never read what David did when he and his companions were in need and hungry? He went into the house of God, when Abiathar was the high priest, and ate the holy bread, which only the priests are permitted to eat, and he even gave some to his companions."

Then Jesus said to them, "The Sabbath was made for people, not people for the Sabbath; therefore, the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath."

On another occasion, he entered a synagogue where there was a man with a shriveled hand. They were watching Jesus closely to see if he would heal him on the Sabbath so they could accuse him of wrongdoing. Jesus said to the man with the shriveled hand, "Come up here and stand in front of everyone." Then he turned to the others and asked, "Is it lawful to do good or harm on the Sabbath? To save a life or to kill? If any of you has a sheep and it falls into a pit on the Sabbath, wouldn't you take hold of it and pull it out? A person is far more valuable than a sheep!" But they had no response. Jesus looked around at them, filled with sorrow and indignation because they lacked compassion, and then said to the man, "Stretch out your hand." He stretched it out, and it was completely healed. But the Pharisees went out and immediately plotted with the Herodians on how they might get rid of him.

title: Jesus Calms The Storm
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Jesus decided to go to the sea with his disciples, and a huge crowd followed him from Galilee. People came from Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, the other side of the Jordan River, and from the regions around Tyre and Sidon. They had heard about his miracles and wanted to see him. To avoid getting overwhelmed by the crowd, Jesus instructed his disciples to prepare a small boat for him. He had healed so many that all the sick and troubled people were trying to reach out and touch him. Whenever those possessed by evil spirits saw him, they fell at his feet and shouted, "You are the Son of God!" However, he repeatedly told them to keep quiet about his identity.

As evening settled in, Jesus said to his disciples, "Let’s cross to the other side of the sea." They left the crowd behind and took him along in the boat, just as he was, and other boats accompanied them. Suddenly, a fierce windstorm arose, and waves crashed into the boat, threatening to sink it. But Jesus was in the stern, fast asleep on a cushion. The disciples shook him awake, saying, "Teacher, don’t you care that we’re going to drown?" He got up, reprimanded the wind, and commanded the sea, "Peace, be still!" Immediately, the wind stopped, and the sea became calm. He looked at them and asked, "Why are you so afraid? Do you still have no faith?" They were amazed and whispered to each other, "Who is this man that even the wind and the sea obey him?"

title: Jesus Heals A Little Girl
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
After crossing the Sea of Galilee in a boat, Jesus found himself surrounded by a large crowd waiting for him on the other side. Among them was Jairus, a synagogue leader, who approached Jesus, fell at his feet, and urgently pleaded, "My little daughter is dying! Please come and lay your hands on her so she can be healed and live!" Moved by his desperation, Jesus agreed to go with him, and a huge crowd followed closely behind.

In that crowd was a woman who had been suffering from constant bleeding for twelve years. She had consulted many doctors and spent all her money on treatments, but instead of getting better, her condition only worsened. When she heard about Jesus, she made her way through the crowd and touched the hem of his robe, thinking to herself, "If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed."

As soon as she touched his garment, the bleeding stopped, and she realized that she had been healed. Jesus, feeling the healing power leave him, turned to the crowd and asked, "Who touched my clothes?" His disciples replied, "You see the crowd pressing around you, and yet you ask, 'Who touched me?'" Still, Jesus kept looking for the person who had touched him. The woman, trembling and frightened, came forward, knowing what had happened, and fell at his feet, sharing her story. Jesus said to her, "Daughter, your faith has healed you. Go in peace, and be free from your suffering."

While Jesus was still speaking, some messengers arrived from Jairus's house with the news, "Your daughter has died. There's no need to bother the teacher any further." But Jesus overheard their words and said to Jairus, "Don't be afraid; just believe."

Jesus took only Peter, James, and John, the brother of James, with him when he went to Jairus's house. There, he found a chaotic scene, with people crying and wailing loudly. As he entered, Jesus asked, "Why are you making such a commotion? The child isn't dead; she's just sleeping." The crowd laughed at him mockingly. He then sent them out and brought only the child's parents and his three disciples into the room where she lay. Taking her hand, he said, "Talitha koumi," which means, "Little girl, get up." To everyone's amazement, the twelve-year-old girl immediately stood up and began walking around. Jesus instructed them sternly to tell no one what had happened and to give her something to eat.

title: Jesus Visits His Old Home
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Jesus returned to Nazareth, the town where he grew up. True to his usual routine, he entered the synagogue on the Sabbath and stood up to read. He was handed the scroll of the prophet Isaiah, and as he spread it open, he found the passage that said:

"The Spirit of the Lord is upon me,\nHe has called me to share good news with the poor,\nHe has sent me to announce freedom to the captives,\nTo restore sight to the blind,\nTo set free those who are crushed by oppression,\nTo declare the year when the Lord will show kindness."

After rolling up the scroll, he gave it back to the attendant and took a seat. Everyone in the synagogue was focused on him, and he declared, "Today, this scripture is fulfilled in your presence."

As he continued to teach in the synagogue, many listeners were amazed and whispered, "Where did he get this knowledge? What is this wisdom given to him? And what about all these remarkable healings he performs? Isn't he just the carpenter, the son of Mary, and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Don't his sisters live here with us?" They struggled to believe in him. Jesus replied, "A prophet is not honored except in his hometown and among his own family."

In that setting, he was only able to perform a few healings by laying hands on some sick people, and he was surprised by their lack of faith. So he traveled to nearby villages to teach.

title: Jesus Calls Together His Friends And Helpers
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Jesus climbed the hillside near Capernaum and called the people he wanted to join him. They came to him, and he chose twelve to be his closest companions and to spread the word, giving them the power to drive out evil spirits. These were the twelve disciples: Simon, who he renamed Peter, James and his brother John, known as the "Sons of Thunder," Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddeus, Simon the Zealot, and Judas Iscariot, who would ultimately betray him.

Afterward, Jesus went into a house, but the crowd gathered again, making it impossible for him even to eat. When his family heard what was happening, they decided to reach out to him, thinking, "He must be out of his mind." While he was surrounded by a crowd, someone told him, "Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside, looking for you." He replied, "Who are my mother and my brothers?" Then, looking at those seated around him, he said, "Here are my mother and my brothers. Whoever does what God wants is my brother, sister, and mother."

title: What Jesus Asks Of His Friends And Helpers
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Jesus visited a village where a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home. Martha had a sister, Mary, who sat at Jesus' feet, completely absorbed in what he was saying. Meanwhile, Martha felt overwhelmed with her duty to serve him, so she approached Jesus and said, "Lord, don't you care that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Please tell her to help me." Jesus replied gently, "Martha, Martha, you’re worried and upset about a lot of things, but really, only a few things matter—actually, just one. Mary has chosen the better path, and it won't be taken away from her."

As Jesus and his disciples walked along a road, someone said to him, "I will follow you wherever you go." Jesus replied, "Foxes have dens, birds have nests, but the Son of Man doesn’t have anywhere to rest his head." Then he spoke to another man, telling him, "Follow me." The man answered, "First, let me go and bury my father." Jesus responded, "Let the dead bury their own dead; you go and announce the Kingdom of God." Another person said, "I will follow you, Lord, but first, I need to say goodbye to my family at home." Jesus replied, "Anyone who looks back after putting their hand to the plow isn’t fit for the Kingdom of God."

While crowds were gathered around him, Jesus turned to them and said, "If anyone wants to come after me, they must be ready to leave behind their parents, spouse, children, and siblings—even their own life. Otherwise, they simply cannot be my disciple. Whoever doesn’t carry their own cross as they follow me can’t be my disciple."

Jesus continued, "Which of you would start building a tower without sitting down first and estimating the cost to see if you have enough money to complete it? If you lay the foundation but can't finish the structure, everyone will laugh at you and say, 'This person started building but couldn't finish!'"

title: The Way To Learn From Jesus
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Jesus taught his disciples, saying, "Don't think I came to dismiss the old laws or the teachings of the prophets. I came to fulfill them, not set them aside."

"Come to me, all of you who are struggling and weighed down, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I'm gentle and compassionate, and you will find rest, because my yoke is easy and my burden is light."

"Whoever hears my words and puts them into practice is like a wise man who built his house on solid rock. The rain poured down, the floods rose, the winds blew and beat against that house; yet it didn’t collapse, because it was built on a strong foundation."

"But whoever hears my words and doesn’t put them into practice is like a foolish man who built his house on sand. The rain fell, the floods came, the winds blew and slammed against that house, and it fell with a great crash."

Another time, when Jesus was teaching by the lake, so many people gathered around him that he got into a boat and sat down while the crowd stayed on the shore. He shared many truths through stories, saying, "Listen carefully. A farmer went out to sow seeds, and as he scattered them, some fell on the path where birds came and ate them. Some fell on rocky ground, where there wasn’t much soil. It sprouted quickly, but when the sun came up, it was scorched because it had no root and withered away. Other seeds fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked the seeds so that they produced nothing. But some fell on good soil, sprouted, grew, and produced a crop – some a hundred times, some sixty, and some thirty times what was sown." And he added, "Anyone who has ears to hear, let them listen!"

When Jesus was alone, those with him and the twelve disciples asked him about the meaning of this story. He said to them, "Don't you understand this parable? How will you grasp any of my other stories? The farmer sows his message. The seeds along the path represent some people who hear, but then Satan comes and snatches away the message that was sown in them."

"The seeds sown on rocky ground represent those who hear the message and receive it with joy, but they have no roots and only remember it for a little while."

"The seeds sown among thorns represent those who hear the message, but the worries of this life and the pursuit of wealth choke it out, and they don’t produce any fruit."

"But the seeds that fall on good soil represent those who hear the message, remember it, and produce a crop; some thirty, some sixty, and some a hundred times what was sown."

"Can a blind person lead another blind person? Won’t both fall into a ditch? A student is not above their teacher, but everyone who is fully trained will be like their teacher."

"A good tree can’t produce bad fruit, nor can a bad tree produce good fruit. Each tree is recognized by its own fruit. People don’t pick figs from thornbushes or grapes from briars. A good person brings good things out of the good stored in their heart, but an evil person brings evil things out of their evil store, for the mouth speaks what the heart is full of."

"You are the light of the world. A city on a hill can’t be hidden. No one lights a lamp and puts it under a bowl; instead, they put it on its stand so it gives light to everyone in the house. Let your light shine before others, so they may see your good deeds and glorify your heavenly Father."

title: The Rewards For Following Jesus' Teachings
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
One day, James and John, the sons of Zebedee, approached Jesus and said, "Master, we have a request. Can you do something for us?" Jesus replied, "What do you want me to do for you?" They said, "When you enter into your glorious kingdom, let one of us sit at your right side and the other at your left." But Jesus responded, "You don’t understand what you’re asking. Can you endure the suffering I will face, or accept the trials I will endure?" They replied, "We can." Jesus told them, "You will experience the suffering I will endure, but the places at my right and left aren’t mine to grant; those spots are reserved for whom they were prepared."

When the other ten disciples heard this, they were initially upset with James and John. But Jesus gathered them and said, "You know that rulers in other nations dominate those beneath them, and their officials command respect. But it shouldn’t be like that among you. If anyone wants to be great, they must serve everyone else; and if anyone wants to be first, they should be the servant of all. I did not come to be served; I came to serve and give my life to bring freedom to many."

Then, Jesus took a young child and placed him among them. He held the child and said, "Whoever welcomes a child like this in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me welcomes not just me, but God who sent me. If anyone gives even a cup of cold water to one of these little ones because they are my follower, I assure you, they will not lose their reward."

"When the Son of Man arrives in glory, with all the angels accompanying him, he will take his place on a magnificent throne, and all people will gather before him. He will sort them, just like a shepherd separates sheep from goats, putting the sheep on his right and the goats on his left."

"Then the King will say to those on his right, 'Come, you who are blessed by my Father, take hold of the kingdom that has been prepared for you since the world began; I was hungry, and you fed me. I was thirsty, and you gave me something to drink. I was a stranger, and you welcomed me. I was without clothes, and you clothed me. I was sick, and you took care of me. I was in prison, and you visited me.'"

"Then the righteous will respond, 'Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you? Or thirsty and give you something to drink? When did we see you as a stranger and welcome you? Or without clothes and clothe you? Or when did we see you sick or in prison and come to you?' The King will reply, 'Truly, I tell you, whatever you did for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you did for me.'"

title: God'S Love Even For Sinners
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Tax collectors and sinners kept coming to Jesus to hear him, which made the Pharisees and scribes complain, "This guy welcomes sinners and even shares meals with them!" So Jesus shared this story: "What man among you, if he has a hundred sheep and loses one, doesn’t leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness to search for the lost sheep until he finds it? And when he finds it, he joyfully places it on his shoulders, and when he gets home, he gathers his friends and says, 'Celebrate with me, because I found my lost sheep.' I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who truly repents than over ninety-nine righteous people who don’t need to change."

"Or what woman, if she has ten silver coins but loses one, wouldn’t light a lamp, sweep the house thoroughly, and search carefully until she finds it? When she finds it, she calls her friends and neighbors and says, 'Celebrate with me, because I found my lost coin!' I tell you, there’s joy among the angels of God over one sinner who truly repents."

Jesus continued, "There was a man with two sons. The younger one said to his father, 'Dad, give me my share of the property.' So the father divided his estate between them. A few days later, the younger son gathered all his belongings and went to a distant country, where he squandered his wealth on wild living. After he had spent everything, a severe famine hit that land, and he began to feel desperate. He ended up working for a local man, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs. He was so hungry that he wished he could fill his belly with the pods the pigs were eating, but no one gave him anything. Finally, he came to his senses and said, 'How many of my father’s hired servants have plenty to eat, while I’m here starving! I will go to my father and say, 'Dad, I’ve sinned against God and you. I no longer deserve to be called your son. Treat me like one of your hired servants.'"

title: God'S Love Even For Sinners
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
"So he got up and went to his father. While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt compassion. He ran to his son, threw his arms around him, and kissed him. The son said, 'Dad, I’ve sinned against God and you. I’m no longer worthy to be called your son.' But the father said to his servants, 'Quick, bring the best robe and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet. Bring the fattened calf and kill it; let’s celebrate! This son of mine was dead, but now he’s alive; he was lost, but now he’s found.' So they began to celebrate.

"Meanwhile, the older son was in the field. As he approached the house, he heard music and dancing. He called one of the servants and asked, 'What’s going on?' The servant replied, 'Your brother has returned, and your father killed the fattened calf because he’s back safe and sound.' The older brother was angry and refused to go in, so his father came out and pleaded with him. But he answered, 'Look, all these years I’ve been working like a slave for you and never disobeyed your commands, yet you never even gave me a young goat to celebrate with my friends. But when this son of yours returns, who wasted your wealth on loose living, you kill the fattened calf for him!' His father said to him, 'Son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours. But we had to celebrate and be glad, because this brother of yours was dead and has come back to life; he was lost but is found.'"

title: God'S Readiness To Answer Prayer
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Jesus said, "Ask, and you'll get what you need; seek, and you'll find what you're looking for; knock, and the door will be opened wide for you. Everyone who asks receives, everyone who seeks finds, and to those who knock, the door will swing open."

"Which of you, if your child asks for a loaf of bread, would give them a stone? Or if they ask for a fish, would give them a snake? If you, being flawed, know how to give good things to your kids, how much more will your Father in heaven give good gifts to those who ask Him?"

"I also want to tell you: if two of you on earth agree and ask for something, it will be granted by my Father in heaven. Because when two or three come together in my name, I am there with them."

title: How To Pray
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
The apostles approached Jesus and said, "Help us build our faith!" Jesus replied, "If you had faith as tiny as a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, 'Get uprooted and plant yourself in the sea,' and it would do it."

He continued, "Have faith in God. I assure you, if anyone tells this hill, 'Jump into the sea,' and truly believes it in their heart without any doubt, it will happen. So, I tell you, believe that whatever you ask for in prayer has already been granted to you, and it will be yours. And when you pray, if someone has wronged you, forgive them so that your Father in heaven can forgive you, too."

One day, after Jesus finished praying in a certain place, one of his disciples asked him, "Lord, teach us how to pray, just like John taught his disciples." Jesus said, "When you pray, say: 'Our Father in heaven, may your name be honored. May your kingdom come, and may your will be done on earth as it is in heaven. Give us today the bread we need, and forgive us our wrongs as we forgive those who wrong us. Help us avoid temptation and rescue us from evil.'"

"When you pray, don't be like the hypocrites who love to stand and pray in synagogues and on street corners to be noticed by others. I tell you, they’ve gotten all the reward they'll ever get! Instead, when you pray, go into your room, shut the door, and pray to your Father who is in secret. Your Father, who sees what’s hidden, will reward you."

"And when you pray, don’t repeat the same phrases over and over like the pagans, who think they’ll be heard just because of their many words. Don't do that. Your Father knows what you need before you even ask Him."

title: Why We Should Trust God
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
"Don't worry about your life—what you'll eat or wear. Isn't life more than food and the body more than clothes? Look at the birds in the sky; they don't plant, harvest, or store away, yet your Heavenly Father feeds them. Aren't you worth much more than they are? Two sparrows only cost a penny, and not a single one falls to the ground without your Father knowing. And as for you, even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid; you are worth far more than sparrows."

"What good does worrying do? Can you add even a single inch to your height by stressing out? And why worry about your clothing? Just look at the lilies in the field—they grow without working or weaving, and yet I tell you, not even Solomon in all his glory was dressed like one of these. If God dresses the grass in the field, which is alive today and thrown into the oven tomorrow, won't He take care of you, you of little faith?"

"So don't stress about what you'll eat, drink, or wear. Those are the things non-believers chase after, but your Heavenly Father knows what you need. Aim to do what’s right, as He wants you to, and all those things will be given to you. So don't worry about tomorrow; it has enough trouble of its own."

title: The Kingdom Of God
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
One day, some little children were brought to Jesus so he could touch them. The disciples were upset with those who brought the kids. When Jesus saw this, he was not pleased and said to his disciples, "Let the little children come to me; don’t stop them, because the Kingdom of God belongs to people like them. I tell you, anyone who doesn’t accept the Kingdom of God like a child will never enter it." Then he picked up the children, held them close, and lovingly blessed them.

On a Sabbath day, Jesus went to have dinner at the home of a prominent Pharisee. One of the guests said to him, "How lucky is the person who gets to share in the Kingdom of God!"

But Jesus replied, "A man hosted a big dinner and invited many guests. When it was time to eat, he sent out his servant to tell those who were invited, 'Come, for everything is ready now.' But one by one, they all started making excuses. The first one said, 'I’ve bought a field and need to go see it. Please excuse me.' Another said, 'I’ve just bought five yoke of oxen and need to try them out. Please excuse me.' Still another said, 'I just got married, so I can’t come.'

The servant returned and told his master what they said. The master of the house was furious and told his servant, 'Go out right away into the streets and alleys of the town, and bring in the poor, the crippled, the blind, and the lame.' The servant reported back, 'Sir, your orders have been carried out, but there’s still room.' The master then said, 'Go out to the highways and country lanes and urge people to come in, so my house will be full; I tell you, none of those who were invited first will taste my dinner.'

One time, as Jesus was walking along the road, a man ran up, knelt before him, and asked, "Good Teacher, what must I do to ensure I have eternal life?" Jesus replied, "Why do you call me good? Only God is truly good. You know the commandments: 'Do not commit adultery. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not give false testimony. Do not be dishonest. Honor your father and mother.'" The man said, "Teacher, I’ve kept all these commands since I was a kid." Jesus looked at him with love and said, "There’s one thing you lack; go, sell everything you have and give the money to the poor, and you’ll have treasure in heaven. Then come and follow me." But when the man heard this, he became sad and walked away, because he had a lot of wealth. Jesus then looked at his disciples and said, "How hard it is for those who are rich to enter the Kingdom of God!" The disciples were amazed at what he said, but Jesus went on, "Children, how difficult it is for those who rely on their riches to enter God's Kingdom. It's easier for a camel to fit through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's Kingdom." They were astonished and asked, "Then who can be saved?" Jesus looked at them and said, "With humans it’s impossible, but not with God; everything is possible with God."

title: The Two Great Commandments
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
One day, a lawyer approached Jesus and asked, "What’s the most important commandment of all?" Jesus replied, "The most important one is this: 'The Lord our God is one Lord, and you must love the Lord your God with all your heart, all your soul, all your mind, and all your strength.'

"The second commandment is this: 'You shall love your neighbor as yourself.' No commandment is greater than these."

The lawyer responded, "Teacher, you’ve answered correctly. There’s only one God, and there is no other. To love Him with all your heart, all your understanding, and all your strength, and to love your neighbor like you love yourself, is far more important than any burnt offerings or sacrifices."

When Jesus noticed that the lawyer had answered wisely, He said, "You are not far from the Kingdom of God."

title: The Rewards Of Modesty And Unselfishness
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
One day, Jesus visited the home of a prominent Pharisee for dinner. As he observed the guests fighting for the best seats at the table, he shared this advice: "When you're invited to a wedding, don't grab the best spot. What if the host has someone more important in mind? He might come over and say, 'Sorry, you need to move for this person,' and you'll feel embarrassed taking the lowest seat instead.

"Instead, when you get invited, take a seat in the lowest spot. That way, when your host sees you, he might say, 'Friend, come up higher!' Then you'll be recognized and honored in front of everyone. Remember, those who elevate themselves will be brought down, but those who stay humble will be honored."

Next, Jesus told his host, "When you throw a dinner, don't just invite your friends, family, or rich neighbors; they can repay you. Instead, invite the poor, the disabled, the lame, and the blind. You’ll be truly blessed because they can’t repay you, and you’ll be rewarded when the righteous come back to life."

Peter chimed in, "But we’ve given up everything to follow you!" Jesus replied, "I assure you, no one who leaves their home, siblings, parents, or land for my sake and for a good purpose will go without reward. They will receive a hundred times more in this life—more homes, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and land, though they will also face challenges. And in the end, those who seem first will end up last, and those who seem last will be first."

title: How To Use Money
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
One day, a man in the crowd said to Jesus, "Teacher, can you tell my brother to share our inheritance with me?" Jesus replied, "Who made me the judge to settle disputes between you two?" Then he turned to the crowd and warned, "Be careful not to let greed for wealth take hold of you, because life isn't about having more stuff than you need."

Then he shared this story: "There was a rich man whose land produced amazing crops. He thought to himself, 'What should I do? I have nowhere to keep all this harvest.' So he said, 'Here’s the plan: I’ll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store my grain and all my goods. Then I’ll tell myself, Now I can relax; I can eat, drink, and enjoy life for many years to come.'

But God spoke to him, saying, 'You foolish man! This very night, your life will be taken from you. Who will get everything you've stored up?' This is how it is for those who hoard wealth for themselves instead of focusing on what truly matters in the eyes of God.

"Don’t store up treasures for yourselves on earth, where moths and rust can destroy, and where thieves can break in and steal. Instead, store up treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust can ruin, and where thieves can’t break in or steal. Because where your treasure is, there your heart will be too."

"No one can serve two masters: you’ll either hate one and love the other, or be devoted to one and despise the other. You can’t worship both God and money."

While Jesus was sitting across from the Temple treasury, he observed how the people were donating money. Many wealthy individuals were putting in large amounts, but a poor widow came along and dropped in two small coins, which were worth less than a penny. He called his disciples over and said, "I assure you, this poor widow has given more than everyone else combined. They contributed out of their abundance, but she, out of her poverty, has given everything she has, even what she needs to live."

title: Different Ways Of Using Talents
author: The Children's Bible
themes: the importance of using one's talents
text_chunk:
"The Kingdom of Heaven is like a man who, before going on a trip, called his servants and entrusted them with his possessions. To one servant, he gave five talents, to another two, and to the last one, he gave one talent, each according to their ability. After that, the man set off on his journey."

"The servant who received five talents immediately went out and traded with them, earning five more talents. Similarly, the servant with two talents made two more. But the servant who got one talent dug a hole in the ground and hid his master's money."

"After a long time, the master returned and settled accounts with his servants. When the servant who had received five talents came back with five more, he said, 'Master, you entrusted me with five talents. Look, I’ve earned five more!' The master replied, 'Well done, good and faithful servant! You've been faithful in a few things; now I will put you in charge of many things. Come and share in your master's joy.'"

"The servant who had received two talents also approached and said, 'Master, you gave me two talents. Here, I’ve earned two more.' His master responded, 'Well done, good and faithful servant! You've been faithful in a few things; now I will put you in charge of many things. Come and share in your master's joy.'"

"Then the servant who received one talent came forward and said, 'Master, I knew you were a tough man, reaping where you haven't sown and gathering where you haven't scattered seed. I was afraid, so I hid your talent in the ground. Here it is, all safe.'"

"But the master replied, 'You lazy and worthless servant! You knew I reap where I haven’t sown and gather where I haven’t scattered seed. You should have put my money in the bank, so when I returned, I would have received it with interest. Take the talent from him and give it to the servant who has ten talents; for to everyone who has, more will be given, and he will have abundance; but from the one who has nothing, even what he has will be taken away. Throw this worthless servant into the darkness outside, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.'"

title: The Good Samaritan
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
One day, a lawyer stood up to test Jesus, asking, "Teacher, what must I do to gain eternal life?" Jesus replied, "What does the law say? How do you interpret it?" The lawyer answered, "You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, soul, strength, and mind; and love your neighbor as yourself." Jesus said, "You’ve answered correctly; do this and you will live."

Wanting to justify himself, he asked Jesus, "Who is my neighbor?" Jesus replied, "A man was traveling from Jerusalem to Jericho when he was attacked by robbers. They stripped him, beat him, and left him half dead. By chance, a priest was heading down the same road. When he saw the man, he avoided him and passed by on the other side.

Similarly, a Levite came to that spot, looked at the man, and also passed by on the other side. But a Samaritan, who was on a journey, came upon the man and felt compassion for him. He went to him, bandaged his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. Then he lifted the man onto his own donkey, brought him to an inn, and took care of him. The next day, he took out two silver coins and gave them to the innkeeper, saying, 'Take care of him, and whatever else you spend, I’ll reimburse you when I return.'

"Which of these three do you think was a neighbor to the man who was attacked?" The lawyer replied, "The one who showed him mercy." Jesus said to him, "Go and do likewise."

title: The Way To Treat Those Who Wrong Us
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
"If your brother wrongs you, talk to him about it when it’s just the two of you. If he listens, you’ve won him back. Even if he messes up seven times in a day, if he turns to you seven times and says, 'I'm sorry,' you should forgive him."

Peter approached Jesus and asked, "Master, how many times should I let my brother wrong me and then forgive him? Seven times?" Jesus replied, "I tell you, not just seven times, but seventy times seven."

"That's why the Kingdom of Heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants. As he started the process, one man was brought to him who owed ten thousand talents. Since he couldn’t pay, the master ordered that he be sold along with his wife, kids, and all his possessions to cover the debt. The servant fell to the ground and pleaded, 'Master, please be patient with me, and I promise I’ll pay everything I owe.' Moved by compassion, the master let him go and forgave his entire debt."

"However, as soon as that servant left, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him one-sixtieth of a talent. He grabbed him by the throat and demanded, 'Pay me what you owe!' The man fell down and begged, 'Please have patience with me, and I’ll repay you.' But the first servant refused and had him thrown in prison until he could pay back the debt."

"When the other servants saw what had happened, they were distressed and reported it to their master. The master called the servant in and said, 'You wicked servant! I forgave you your entire debt because you begged me. Shouldn’t you have shown the same mercy to your fellow servant that I showed to you?' Out of anger, the master handed him over to the jailers until he could pay back all he owed. This is how my heavenly Father will treat you unless you truly forgive your brother from your heart.'

title: The People Who Are Really Happy
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Jesus spoke to his followers:

"Happy are those who are humble, for they will be part of the Kingdom of Heaven. Happy are the gentle, for they will inherit the earth. Happy are those who grieve, for they will find comfort. Happy are those who crave justice, for they will be satisfied. Happy are the compassionate, for they will receive compassion. Happy are those with pure hearts, for they will see God. Happy are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God. Happy are those who are treated badly for doing what’s right, for theirs is the Kingdom of Heaven. Happy are you when people insult you, mistreat you, and tell lies about you because you stand by me; celebrate and be joyful, because your reward in heaven is great, just like the prophets who came before you!"

title: Jesus Sends Out The First Missionaries
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Jesus traveled through all the towns and villages, teaching in their synagogues, spreading the good news about the Kingdom of God, and healing various kinds of illnesses and afflictions. When he saw the crowds, confused and lost like sheep without a shepherd, he felt deep compassion. He said to his disciples, "The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few. Pray to the Lord of the harvest to send workers into His fields."

Then, he called the twelve disciples and sent them out in pairs, giving them authority over evil spirits. He instructed them to take nothing for their journey except a staff. "Set out on your way," he said. "Remember that I'm sending you out like lambs among wolves. Don't take a purse, a bag, or an extra pair of shoes, and don’t stop to chat with anyone on the road. When you enter a household, say, 'Peace to this house!' If a person in that house is deserving, your peace will rest on him; if not, it will return to you. Stay in the same house, eating and drinking what they offer you, for the worker deserves his wages. Don’t move from house to house."

"In whatever town you go, if the people welcome you, eat what they give you. Heal the sick in that town and tell them, 'The Kingdom of God is near you.' But if you enter a town and the people do not receive you, go out into the streets and declare, 'Even the dust from your town that sticks to our feet, we shake off as a protest against you.' But remember, the Kingdom of God is at hand. I tell you, it will be better on that day for Sodom than for that city."

"Don’t give what is sacred to dogs, and don't throw your pearls before pigs, or they may trample them underfoot and turn back to attack you. Whoever listens to you listens to me; whoever rejects you rejects me; and whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me."

So, the disciples went out and preached, encouraging people to feel remorse for their sins and to live rightly. They also cast out many evil spirits and healed numerous sick individuals by anointing them with oil. When they returned to Jesus, they shared everything they had done and taught.

title: Jesus Praises John The Baptist
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
When John heard about what Jesus was doing while he was in prison, he sent his disciples to ask him, "Are you the one we’ve been waiting for, or should we be looking for someone else?" Jesus replied, "Go back and tell John what you see and hear: the blind can see, the lame are walking, lepers are being healed, the deaf can hear, the dead are being raised, and the good news is being proclaimed to the poor. Blessed is anyone who doesn’t lose faith in me."

As John's disciples left, Jesus turned to the crowd and spoke about John: "What did you go into the wilderness to see? A flimsy reed waving in the wind? So what did you expect? Someone dressed in fine clothes? People who wear fancy clothes live in palaces. But why did you go out there? To see a prophet? Yes, I tell you, and even more than a prophet! This is the one about whom it is written:

"'Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare the way for you.'

"I tell you, no one has been born who is greater than John the Baptist; yet, even the least in the Kingdom of God is greater than he."

"How can I describe the people of this time? They're like kids sitting in the marketplace, calling out to their friends and saying:

"'We played music for you, but you didn’t dance; we sang sad songs, but you didn’t cry.'

"John didn't eat or drink, and people said, 'He must have a demon!' The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, 'Look at him! He’s a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!' But what I do shows that I am wise and right."

When Herod's birthday came, the daughter of Herodias performed a dance that pleased him. He promised with an oath to give her anything she wanted. Guided by her mother, she asked, "I want the head of John the Baptist on a plate." Even though the king didn't want to do it, he reluctantly ordered it to be done because of his oath and the guests he had. He commanded that John be beheaded in prison, and they brought his head on a plate and gave it to the girl, who then gave it to her mother. Later, John's disciples came and took the body away to bury it.

title: Jesus Feeds The Hungry
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
When Herod, the ruler of Galilee, heard about what Jesus was doing, he was really confused. Some people said that John the Baptist had come back from the dead, others claimed that Elijah had appeared, and still, others thought one of the old prophets had returned. Herod wondered, "I beheaded John; who is this person I'm hearing about?" He wanted to find out more.

Then Jesus said to his disciples, "Let’s go to a quiet place and take a break. There are so many people coming and going that you guys haven’t even had a chance to eat!" So they hopped into a boat and headed for a peaceful spot, but many people recognized them and ran ahead from all the towns to meet them. When Jesus got out of the boat, he found a huge crowd waiting for him. He felt compassion for them because they were like sheep without a shepherd, so he began teaching them many things.

As the day got late, the disciples came to him and said, "This place is remote and it's already getting late. Send the people to the nearby farms and villages to buy food for themselves." But Jesus replied, "You give them something to eat." They said, "Are we supposed to go and spend two hundred silver coins on food for all these people?" He told them, "Go see how many loaves of bread you have." When they checked, they reported, "We have five loaves and two fish." Then he instructed them to have the people sit down on the green grass.

So they settled down in groups of a hundred and fifty. Jesus took the five loaves and two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed the loaves, and broke them into pieces. He gave the pieces to the disciples to distribute to the crowd. He also divided the two fish among them, and everyone ate until they were full. Afterwards, they collected twelve baskets of leftovers, even though about five thousand people had shared the meal.

Afterward, Jesus directed his disciples to get into the boat and go ahead of him to Bethsaida while he sent the crowd away. Once he had dismissed them, he climbed a mountain to pray. By evening, the boat was in the middle of the sea, and Jesus was alone on land. He noticed that they were struggling against the wind while rowing, so at about three o'clock in the morning, he walked over to them on the water as if he meant to pass by. When the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they thought he was a ghost and screamed in fright; all of them were terrified. But he quickly called out, "Take courage, it’s me; don’t be afraid." Then he got into the boat with them, and the wind calmed down. They were amazed, for they hadn’t understood the lesson of the loaves, and their hearts were hardened.

Once they crossed the sea, they landed at Gennesaret and tied the boat up. As soon as they stepped ashore, the people recognized Jesus and spread the word all over that region.
