Whenever he was spotted in a certain area, crowds brought their sick on beds. In every town, city, or village he visited, people laid their sick in the marketplaces, begging him to let them touch even the hem of his garment. Everyone who touched him was healed.

title: Jesus Tells What It Means To Be Clean
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Some Pharisees and scribes from Jerusalem came to see Jesus because they noticed that some of his disciples ate without washing their hands first, which the scribes thought was necessary. The Pharisees and all Jews typically wash their hands up to their wrists before meals. So, the Pharisees and scribes asked him, "Why don’t your disciples follow the old custom and eat with clean hands?" Jesus replied, "Isaiah was right about you hypocrites: 'These people honor me with their words, but their hearts are far from me; their worship is meaningless because they teach human rules instead of God’s commands.' You ignore God’s command and choose human traditions instead.

"Moses said, 'Honor your father and your mother,' and, 'Anyone who speaks ill of their father or mother must die.' But you say, 'If someone tells their father or mother that what they would have received from me is dedicated to God,' then they don’t have to do anything for their parents. In doing this, you ignore God’s command for the traditions you’ve passed down, and you do many other things like this too."

Then, he called the crowd back to him and said, "Listen up, everyone! Understand this: nothing that goes into a person from the outside can make them unclean. It’s what comes out of a person that makes them unclean. From within, from the heart, come evil thoughts, theft, murder, greed, wickedness, deceit, impure desires, envy, slander, arrogance, and foolishness. All these evil things come from within, and they make a person unclean."

title: Jesus Makes An Heroic Choice
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
As they traveled toward the villages of Cæsarea Philippi, Jesus asked his disciples, "Who do people say that I am?" They replied, "Some say you're John the Baptist; others say you're Elijah or one of the prophets." Then he asked them, "But what about you? Who do you say I am?" Peter answered, "You are the Christ." Jesus responded firmly, telling them not to share with anyone that he was the Christ.

Next, Jesus started to explain to his disciples that he would face a lot of suffering, be rejected by the elders, high priests, and scribes, and ultimately be killed. But he also told them that after three days, he would rise from the dead. He spoke openly about this, but Peter tried to correct him. Jesus turned to Peter and said, "Get behind me, Satan! You don't have your mind on the things of God but on human concerns."

Then Jesus called the crowd along with his disciples and said, "If anyone wants to follow me, they must deny themselves, take up their cross, and follow me. Anyone who tries to save their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life for my sake and the sake of the good news will save it. What good is it for someone to gain the whole world yet lose their soul? What could anyone give in exchange for their soul? If someone is ashamed of me and my teachings in this sinful world, I will be ashamed of them when I come in the glory of my Father with the holy angels."

title: Jesus Is Glorified
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Six days later, Jesus took Peter, James, and John up a high mountain where they could be alone. In front of them, he was transformed; his clothes shone with a brilliant whiteness that no earthly bleach could achieve. Suddenly, Elijah and Moses appeared and began to talk with Jesus. Peter, overwhelmed, said, "Master, it's great that we're here! Let’s set up three shelters—one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah." He didn’t really know what to say because he was scared. Then, a cloud descended, covering them, and a voice came from the cloud, saying, "This is my Beloved Son; listen to him." When they looked around, they saw no one except Jesus.

As they were heading down the mountain, Jesus instructed them, "Don't tell anyone what you’ve seen until after I rise from the dead." They complied, but debated among themselves what "rising from the dead" could mean. So, they asked him, "Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?" Jesus replied, "Elijah is meant to come first to set everything right. And what does it say about the Son of Man? Isn’t it that he will suffer greatly and be rejected? But I assure you, Elijah has already come, and they treated him however they wanted, just as it was written about him."

title: Jesus Tells How One May Become Great
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
As Jesus and his disciples traveled through Galilee after leaving Cæsarea Philippi, he wanted to keep their journey a secret because he was teaching them. He told them, "The Son of Man will be betrayed and people will kill him, but three days later, he will rise from the dead." However, they didn't grasp what he meant and were too afraid to ask.

Once they arrived in Capernaum and were in the house, Jesus asked, "What were you arguing about on the way?" They stayed silent because they had been discussing which of them was the greatest. Jesus sat down, called the twelve disciples to him, and said, "If anyone wants to be first, they must be the last and serve everyone else." Then he took a little child and stood him next to him. With his arm around the child, he added, "Whoever welcomes a little child like this in my name welcomes me, and whoever welcomes me also welcomes not just me, but the God who sent me."

title: Jesus Makes The Wicked Zaccheus His Friend
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
While Jesus was passing through Jericho, a man named Zaccheus—who was the chief tax collector and quite wealthy—wanted to see what Jesus was like. However, because he was short and there was a large crowd, he couldn't get a glimpse of him. So, he ran ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree to catch sight of Jesus as he passed by. When Jesus reached that spot, he looked up and called out, "Zaccheus, come down! I need to stay at your house today." Zaccheus quickly descended and welcomed him with joy.

When the crowd saw this, they began to complain, saying, "He’s gone to be a guest with a man who is a sinner." But Zaccheus stood up and said to Jesus, "Lord, I’ll give half of my possessions to the poor, and if I've cheated anyone, I’ll pay them back four times the amount." Jesus replied, "Today salvation has come to this house, because you have shown yourself to be a true descendant of Abraham. The Son of Man came to seek and save what was lost."

title: Jesus Is Gladly Welcomed By The People
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
As Jesus was leaving Jericho with his disciples and a large crowd trailing behind, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, son of Timaeus, sat by the roadside. When he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth passing by, he shouted, "Jesus, son of David, have mercy on me!" Many people told him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, "Son of David, have mercy on me!" Jesus stopped and said, "Call him over." They called the blind man and said, "Take heart! Get up, He’s calling for you." Bartimaeus threw off his cloak and jumped up to go to Jesus. Jesus asked him, "What do you want me to do for you?" The blind man replied, "Rabbi, I want to see again." Jesus said, "Go, your faith has healed you." Immediately, he regained his sight and began to follow Jesus along the road.

As Jesus and His followers were approaching Jerusalem, they reached Bethpage and Bethany, near the Mount of Olives. He sent two of His disciples ahead, telling them, "Go to the village over there. As soon as you enter, you’ll find a colt tied up that hasn’t been ridden by anyone. Untie it and bring it here. If anyone asks you, 'Why are you untying that colt?' say, 'The Master needs it and will send it back right away.'"

The disciples went and found the colt tied up outside a house at the street. As they were untying it, some bystanders asked, "What are you doing, untying that colt?" They replied with the message Jesus gave them, and the men let them take it. After bringing the colt to Jesus, they placed their cloaks on it, and He got on. Many spread their clothes on the road, while others laid down leafy branches they had cut from the fields. The crowd in front and behind started shouting:

"Hosanna!
Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord!
Blessed is the coming Kingdom of our ancestor David!
Hosanna in the highest!"

When Jesus entered Jerusalem, He went into the Temple. After looking around at everything since it was already late, He left for Bethany with His twelve disciples.

title: Jesus Fights Wrong In The Temple
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Around this time, some people came to tell Jesus about the Galileans whom Pilate had killed while they were offering sacrifices. He replied, "Do you think these Galileans were worse sinners than everyone else? No, I tell you; unless you feel regret for your sins and do what’s right, you will all perish too. And what about those eighteen people who died when the tower of Siloam fell? Do you think they were more sinful than everyone else in Jerusalem? No, I tell you; unless you truly regret your sins and change your ways, you will also perish."

Then Jesus entered the Temple and drove out everyone who was buying and selling there. He knocked over the tables of the money-changers and the chairs of those selling doves, and he wouldn’t let anyone carry goods through the Temple. He said, "Isn’t it written, 'My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations'? But you’ve turned it into a hideout for thieves!" The chief priests and scribes, upon hearing this, started looking for a way to kill him because they were afraid of him; all the people were captivated by his teachings. Each evening, he and his disciples left the city.

Later, Jesus and his disciples re-entered Jerusalem, and as he was walking around the Temple, some high priests, scribes, and elders approached him and asked, "What authority do you have to do these things, and who gave you this authority?" Jesus responded, "I’ll ask you a question; answer me, and then I’ll tell you by what authority I do these things. What about John’s baptism? Was it from God or from humans? Answer me." They debated among themselves, saying, "If we say, 'From God,' he’ll ask, 'Then why didn’t you believe him?' But if we say, 'From men'"—they were worried about what the people would say since the people viewed John as a true prophet. So they replied to Jesus, "We don’t know." Jesus then told them, "Then I won’t tell you by what authority I do these things."

title: Jesus Fights Wrong In The Temple
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
"Let me ask you this: A man had two sons. He went to the first and said, 'Son, go work in the vineyard today.' The young man replied, 'I won't go,' but later he had a change of heart and went. Then the father approached the other son and said the same thing. The second son replied, 'Sure, I will go, sir,' but he didn’t go. Which of the two did what their father wanted?" They answered, "The first one." Jesus said to them, "I tell you, tax collectors and sinners will enter the Kingdom of God before you. John showed you the path to a good life, but you didn’t believe him. Tax collectors and sinners did believe him; and even when you saw this, you refused to admit you were wrong and believe in him."

"Listen closely: There was a vineyard owner who planted a vineyard, fenced it in, dug out a wine-press, and built a watchtower. Then he rented it out to some tenants and went on a journey to another country. When it was grape harvest time, he sent his servants to the tenants to collect his share of the fruit. But the tenants beat one servant, stoned another, and killed a third. He sent even more servants, but they treated them the same way. Finally, he sent his son, thinking, 'They’ll respect my son.' But the tenants saw him coming and said to each other, 'This is the heir. Let’s kill him and take his inheritance.' So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. Now, what do you think the vineyard owner will do when he comes back?" They replied, "He’ll destroy those wicked tenants and lease the vineyard to others who will give him the fruits at harvest time."

Jesus then said to them, "Have you never read the scriptures:

"'The stone the builders rejected
Has become the cornerstone;
The Lord has done this,
And it’s amazing in our eyes.'"

When the high priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they realized he was talking about them, and they wanted to arrest him, but they were afraid of the crowds, who considered him a prophet.

title: Jesus Condemns Those Who Pretend To Be Good
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
The crowd was listening to Jesus with excitement. While he taught, he warned them, "Watch out for the scribes! They love to walk around in long robes and expect people to bow down to them in the marketplaces. They enjoy sitting in the best seats at synagogues and at feasts. These are the ones who take advantage of widows' properties, and to hide their wrongdoings, they pray long prayers. They'll face the harshest judgment."

He continued, "Woe to you, scribes! You put heavy burdens on people that you yourself won’t even touch with a finger. Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You block the way to the Kingdom of God for others; you don’t enter yourselves and you don’t allow those who want to enter to do so."

"Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You meticulously give a tenth of your garden’s produce to the Temple, but you ignore justice, mercy, and faithfulness. You’re like blind guides who filter out a tiny gnat but swallow a whole camel!"

"Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You clean the outside of a cup and plate but fill them with greed and selfishness. You blind Pharisee! Start by cleaning the inside of the cup, so the outside will also be clean."

"Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You’re like whitewashed tombs, looking beautiful from the outside, but inside you’re full of the bones of the dead and all kinds of filth. You appear righteous, but inside you’re full of hypocrisy and sin."

title: Jesus Warns His Disciples
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
As Jesus was leaving the Temple, one of his disciples exclaimed, "Master, look at the huge stones and the stunning building!" Jesus replied, "This Temple, built by human hands, will be torn down. But a new one will rise that won't be made by hands at all." Then, as he settled on the Mount of Olives facing the Temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew privately asked him, "So when will all of this happen, and what signs should we look for to know it's coming?" Jesus answered them, "No one knows the day or the hour of this event—not even the angels in heaven or the Son—only the Father knows."

"The Kingdom of God is like ten maidens who took their torches and went out to greet the bridegroom. Five were foolish, and five were wise. The foolish ones took their torches but didn’t bring any oil, while the wise ones packed oil in their vessels along with their torches."

"While the bridegroom was delayed, they all fell asleep. At midnight, a shout went out: 'Look! The bridegroom is here! Come out to meet him!' All the maidens woke up and prepared their torches. The foolish ones said to the wise, 'Please give us some of your oil; our torches are going out.' The wise maidens replied, 'We can't share our oil; there might not be enough for both us and you. Go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.' While they went off to buy oil, the bridegroom arrived, and those who were ready went in with him to the wedding feast. The door was shut. Later, the other maidens came back and called out, 'Sir, open up for us!' But he replied, 'I truly don’t know you.'

"So stay alert, because you don’t know the day or hour when the Kingdom of God will come."

title: Jesus Tells What He Came To Do
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Jesus told the crowd, "I'm the light of the world. Anyone who follows me won't walk in darkness; instead, they'll have the light that gives life."

As Jesus shared these words, many people started to believe in him. Then he turned to the Jewish believers and said, "If you truly follow what I teach, you are my real disciples, and you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free." They replied, "We're descendants of Abraham and have never been anyone's slaves. What do you mean by saying, 'You will be set free'?" Jesus responded, "I assure you, everyone who sins is a slave to sin. A slave doesn't stay in the house forever, but the Son stays forever. So if the Son sets you free, you will be truly free."

"I am the Door; if anyone enters through me, he will be saved and will go in and out, finding pasture. The thief comes only to steal, kill, and destroy. I’ve come so they can have life, and have it abundantly."

"I am the Good Shepherd; a good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep. But a hired hand, who isn't a shepherd and doesn't own the sheep, runs away when he sees a wolf coming. The wolf attacks the sheep and scatters them. The hired hand runs off because he’s just that—a hired hand who doesn’t care about the sheep."

"I am the Good Shepherd, and I know my sheep, and they know me, just as the Father knows me and I know the Father. I lay down my life for the sheep. I have other sheep that don’t belong to this fold; I need to lead them too, and they will hear my voice. Then there will be one flock with one shepherd."

title: Jesus Brings Lazarus Back To Life
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
There was a man named Lazarus from Bethany, who was really sick. He was the brother of Martha and Mary, the woman who anointed Jesus with perfume and wiped his feet with her hair. Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus. So the sisters sent a message to him, saying, "Master, the one you love is sick." When Jesus heard this, he replied, "This sickness won't end in death; it's for the glory of God so that the Son of God can be glorified through it."

When Jesus found out that Lazarus was sick, he stayed where he was for two more days. Then he told his disciples, "Let’s go back to Judea. Our friend Lazarus has fallen asleep, but I’m going to wake him up." The disciples responded, "Master, if he's asleep, he'll recover." They didn't realize that Jesus was talking about Lazarus's death. So Jesus made it clear, "Lazarus is dead, and I’m glad I wasn’t there for your sake so that you can believe. Now let’s go to him."

By the time Jesus arrived, Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days. Bethany was only about two miles from Jerusalem, and many Jews had come to comfort Mary and Martha over their brother.

When Martha heard that Jesus was approaching, she went out to greet him while Mary stayed at home. Martha said to Jesus, "Master, if you had been here, my brother wouldn't have died, but I know that even now, God will give you whatever you ask for." Jesus replied, "Your brother will rise again." Martha responded, "I know he will rise again in the resurrection on the last day." Jesus assured her, "I am the resurrection and the life. Anyone who believes in me will live even if they die, and whoever lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this?" She replied, "Yes, Master, I believe that you are the Christ, the Son of God, who is to come into the world."

title: Jesus Brings Lazarus Back To Life
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
After this, Martha went back to secretly tell Mary, "The Master is here and is asking for you." When Mary heard this, she quickly got up and went to him. Jesus had not yet entered the village, but was still at the spot where Martha had met him. The Jews who were comforting Mary saw her get up quickly and followed her, thinking she was going to cry at the tomb. When Mary reached Jesus and saw him, she fell at his feet and said, "Master, if you had been here, my brother wouldn't have died."

When Jesus saw her and the Jews weeping with her, he was deeply moved and asked, "Where have you laid him?" They replied, "Master, come and see." Jesus wept. The Jews commented, "Look how he loved him!" Yet, some asked, "Couldn’t this man who opened the eyes of the blind also have kept Lazarus from dying?"

Jesus was once more deeply moved as he approached the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone was laid across it. Jesus said, "Take away the stone." Martha, who was the sister of the dead man, told him, "Master, he’s been dead for four days now; there will be a smell." Jesus responded, "Didn’t I tell you that if you believed, you would see the glory of God?" So they took away the stone, and Jesus looked up and said, "Father, I thank you that you have heard me. I knew that you always hear me, but I said this for the sake of the people standing here, so that they may believe that you sent me." After saying this, he shouted in a loud voice, "Lazarus, come out!" The dead man came out, his hands and feet wrapped in strips of linen, and a cloth was around his face. Jesus said to them, "Unbind him and let him go."

Many of the Jews who had come to visit Mary and had seen what Jesus did, believed in him.

title: Jesus Praises A Woman Who Gave Her Best
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
While Jesus was having dinner at Simon's house in Bethany, a woman entered carrying an alabaster jar filled with expensive perfume. She broke the jar and poured the fragrance over his head. A few bystanders exchanged glances, saying, "What a waste of perfume! It could have been sold for more than three hundred silver coins, and the money could have gone to the poor."

But when they criticized her, Jesus responded, "Leave her alone. Why are you bothering her? She has done something beautiful for me. You will always have the poor among you, and you can help them anytime, but you won't always have me here. She has done what she could by anointing my body in preparation for burial. I assure you, wherever the good news is shared around the world, what she has done will be remembered."

title: Jesus Eats The Last Supper With His Disciples
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve disciples, went to the high priests with the plan to betray Jesus. When they heard his intention, they were thrilled and promised him money. And so, he started looking for a chance to carry out his betrayal.

On the first day of the Feast of Unleavened Bread, when the Jews were sacrificing the lambs for the Passover Feast, Jesus' disciples asked him, "Where do you want us to prepare for your Passover meal?"

Jesus sent two of his disciples, saying, "Go into the city. There, you’ll meet a man carrying a jar of water. Follow him and tell the owner of the house he enters, 'The Master says, Where is my room for the Passover meal with my disciples?' He will show you a spacious upper room that's already set up. Prepare the meal there for us." So the disciples went into the city and found everything just as he described; they got ready for the Passover.

That evening, Jesus arrived with his twelve disciples. While they were eating at the table, he said, "I tell you the truth, one of you sitting here with me will betray me." The disciples were filled with sorrow and asked him one by one, "Surely, it’s not me?" Jesus replied, "It's one of the twelve, the one who is dipping his hand into the bowl with me. The Son of Man will go as it has been predicted, but woe to the one who betrays him! It would be better for that man if he had never been born!"

Then Jesus took the bread, gave thanks to God, broke it, and said, "This is my body, which is broken for you. Do this in memory of me."

After eating, Jesus took the cup, gave thanks to God, and passed it to his disciples, who all drank from it. He said, "This is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for many. Whenever you drink from this cup, do it in memory of me." After they finished singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

There, Jesus told them, "You will all abandon me, because it is written in the scriptures: 'I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.' But after I rise, I will go ahead of you into Galilee." Peter insisted, "Even if everyone else deserts you, I won’t!" Jesus replied, "I assure you, tonight before the rooster crows, you will deny three times that you know me." But Peter declared more strongly, "Even if I have to die with you, I will never deny you!" And all the others said the same thing.

title: Jesus Encourages His Friends And Helpers
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Jesus said, "Don't let your hearts be troubled; believe in God, and believe in me too. In my Father’s house, there are many rooms. If that weren't true, I would have told you. I'm going to prepare a place for you. And when I go and prepare a place for you, I will come back and take you with me, so you can be where I am. You already know the way to the place I'm going."

Thomas replied, "Lord, we don’t know where you’re going, so how can we know the way?" Jesus answered, "I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me. If you had truly known me, you would have known my Father as well. From now on, you do know him and have seen him."

Philip said, "Lord, show us the Father, and that will be enough for us." Jesus replied, "Have I been with you all this time, Philip, and still you don’t know me? Anyone who has seen me has seen the Father. So how can you say, 'Show us the Father'? Don’t you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words I speak are not just my own; they come from the Father who lives in me. Believe me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in me—or at least believe because of the miracles themselves. I assure you, anyone who believes in me will do the same works I have done, and even greater things, because I’m going to the Father. Whatever you ask in my name, I will do, so that the Father may be glorified through the Son. If you ask anything in my name, I will do it."

"If you love me, you will keep my commands. I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Helper to be with you forever, even the Spirit of truth."

"In a little while, the world won’t see me anymore; but you will see me. Because I live, you will live too. Anyone who has my commands and obeys them loves me, and anyone who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and show myself to them."

title: Jesus Encourages His Friends And Helpers
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
"I’ve shared all this while I’m still with you, but the Helper, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything and remind you of all I’ve said to you."

"I’m leaving you with a gift—peace of mind and heart. The peace I give isn’t like what the world gives. So don’t let your hearts be troubled or afraid. You’ve heard me say I’m going away and coming back to you. If you love me, you’ll be happy that I’m going to the Father, because the Father is greater than I am. I’ve told you this now so that when it happens, you will believe."

"I am the true vine, and my Father is the vine-grower. He cuts off every branch that doesn’t produce fruit, and he prunes the branches that do bear fruit so they can produce even more. You are already clean because of the message I've spoken to you. Stay united with me, and I will stay united with you. Just as a branch can’t bear fruit unless it stays connected to the vine, you can’t bear fruit unless you stay connected to me. I am the vine; you are the branches. If you stay connected to me, and I to you, you will produce much fruit; apart from me, you can do nothing."

"If you stay connected to me and my words stay in you, ask for whatever you wish, and it will be done for you. By producing much fruit, you show that you are my true disciples, and this brings glory to my Father. Just as the Father has loved me, I have also loved you; remain in my love. If you keep my commands, you will remain in my love, just as I have kept my Father’s commands and remain in his love."

"I’ve told you all this so that my joy may be in you and your joy may be complete. Here’s my command: Love one another just as I have loved you. There’s no greater love than to lay down one’s life for one’s friends. You are my friends if you do what I command. I no longer call you servants, because a servant doesn’t know what their master is doing. Instead, I call you friends, because I’ve shared everything I heard from my Father with you. You didn’t choose me; I chose you and appointed you to bear lasting fruit, so that whatever you ask the Father in my name, he will give you."

title: Jesus Is Seized By The Mob
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
When Jesus and his disciples arrived at a place called Gethsemane, he instructed them, "Stay here while I pray." However, he took Peter, James, and John with him. Overcome with deep sorrow, he told them, "My heart is filled with sadness. Please stay here and keep watch for me." He then moved a bit farther away, fell to the ground, and prayed, hoping to be spared from the agony he was about to face, saying, "Father, with you, anything is possible. Take this cup of suffering away from me. But not my will, rather your will be done."

When he returned, he found his disciples asleep. He asked Peter, "Simon, are you asleep? Couldn’t you stay awake for just one hour? Stay alert and pray so you don’t fall into temptation. The spirit is willing, but the flesh is weak." He went away again to pray the same prayer. Upon his return, he discovered they were still asleep and very drowsy, unsure of how to respond to him. He came back a third time and said, "You can keep sleeping now and take your rest. It's enough; the hour has come. The Son of Man is being betrayed into the hands of wicked men. Get up; let’s go; here comes the one who betrays me."

While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, arrived, leading a crowd armed with swords and clubs, sent by the high priests, scribes, and elders. Judas had arranged a sign: "The one I kiss is the man. Arrest him and take him away quietly." As soon as he arrived, he approached Jesus and said, "Rabbi," then kissed him. They seized Jesus and arrested him, but one of those with Jesus drew his sword and struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his ear. Jesus turned and said, "Have you come out with swords and clubs to capture me like a criminal? I've been with you every day teaching in the Temple, yet you didn’t arrest me then."

Then, all of Jesus’ disciples abandoned him and fled. However, one young man followed closely behind, only wearing a linen cloth. When the mob tried to grab him, he left the linen behind and ran away naked.

The mob took Jesus away and brought him to the house of the high priest. Peter followed at a distance, and when they had built a fire in the middle of the courtyard and settled in, he sat down among them. A young woman noticed him by the firelight and said, "This man was also with him." But Peter denied it, saying, "I don’t know him, woman." A little later, another person spotted Peter and said, "You’re one of them too!" But he replied, "I am not."

About an hour later, another man said, "Surely this guy was with Jesus, for he’s a Galilean." But Peter said, "I don’t know what you’re talking about!" Just then, while he was still speaking, the rooster crowed. Jesus turned and looked directly at Peter. Then Peter remembered Jesus' words: "Before the rooster crows today, you will deny me three times." Overcome with guilt, Peter went outside and cried bitterly.

title: Jesus In The Hands Of His Enemies
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
The men who grabbed Jesus mocked him and whipped him. They blindfolded him and said, "Hey, Prophet, tell us who hit you!" They hurled many other insults at him.

At dawn, they brought him before the council, where the elders, chief priests, and scribes had gathered. They tried to gather evidence to condemn him to death, but couldn’t find any. Although many made false claims, their stories didn’t match. A few men stood up and lied, saying, "We heard him say, 'I will destroy this temple built by humans, and in three days, I will build another one not made by hands.'" But even these statements didn’t align.

Then the high priest stood up and asked Jesus, "Aren’t you going to answer? What about the accusations these men are making against you?" Jesus remained silent and didn’t respond. The high priest pressed him, "Are you the Christ? If you are, please tell us." Jesus said, "If I tell you, you won’t believe me, and if I ask you questions, you won’t answer. But from now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of God Almighty." Then they all asked, "So you are the Son of God?" He answered, "You say so; I am." They responded, "What further evidence do we need? We’ve heard it straight from his mouth."

Then all the high priests and scribes stood up and took Jesus to Pilate, accusing him, "We found this man misleading our people, telling them not to pay taxes to the Roman emperor, and claiming to be Christ, the King." Pilate asked him, "Are you the King of the Jews?" Jesus replied, "Yes, I am." Pilate told the high priests and the crowd, "I find nothing wrong with this man." But they insisted, shouting, "He’s causing unrest by teaching throughout all Judea. He started in Galilee and has made his way here." When Pilate heard this, he asked if Jesus was a Galilean, and once he found out that he was, and that he fell under Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod Antipas, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.

Herod was excited to see Jesus. He had wanted to meet him for a long time due to all the stories he’d heard, and he hoped to watch him perform some amazing miracle. Even though Herod asked him many questions, Jesus didn’t respond, and the high priests and scribes loudly pressed their charges against him. After mocking Jesus and dressing him in a bright robe, Herod sent him back to Pilate.

Pilate then summoned the high priests, other officials, and the crowd. He said, "You brought me this man, claiming he was stirring up the people to rebel. I’ve examined him in front of you and found no guilt in him for the things you accuse him of; nor has Herod, since he sent him back to us. Clearly, he has done nothing deserving of death. So, I will have him flogged and then release him" (since it was customary during the feast to release one prisoner). But they all shouted, "Away with him! Release Barabbas!" (a man who had been imprisoned for a riot and murder). Pilate tried to speak to them again, wishing to release Jesus, but they yelled, "Crucify him! Crucify him!" For the third time, he asked, "What crime has this man committed? I haven’t found any reason to put him to death. I will have him flogged and then release him." But they shouted louder, demanding that he be crucified. So, Pilate, wanting to please the people, released Barabbas and handed Jesus over to be crucified.

title: Jesus Is Crucified By His Enemies
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
The soldiers took Jesus to the governor's palace courtyard and gathered everyone together. They put a purple robe on him and made a crown of thorns to set on his head, cheering, "Hail, King of the Jews!" They hit him on the head with a stick, spat on him, and knelt before him in mock respect. After they finished ridiculing him, they took off the purple robe, dressed him in his own clothes, and led him away to be crucified.

They forced a man named Simon from Cyrene, who was arriving from the countryside, to carry Jesus' cross. They brought Jesus to a place called Golgotha, which means the place of the skull. They offered him wine mixed with myrrh, but he wouldn’t accept it. Then they crucified him and divided his clothes by casting lots to decide who got what. It was nine in the morning when they crucified him, and the sign above his head stated the charge against him.

Alongside him, they crucified two thieves, one on his right and one on his left. Those passing by mocked him, shaking their heads and saying, "Ha! You said you could destroy the Temple and rebuild it in three days—save yourself and come down from the cross!" The high priests and scribes also sneered at him, saying among themselves, "He saved others, but he can’t save himself. Let the Christ, the 'King of Israel,' come down from the cross now, and we’ll believe in him!" But Jesus said, "Father, forgive them, for they don’t know what they’re doing."

One of the criminals being crucified mocked Jesus, saying, "Aren’t you the Christ? Save yourself and us!" But the other criminal rebuked him, saying, "Have you no fear of God, even when you’re about to die? We’re getting what we deserve for our crimes, but he hasn’t done anything wrong." Then he said, "Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom." Jesus replied, "Today, you will be with me in paradise."

title: Jesus Is Crucified By His Enemies
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Standing near the cross of Jesus was his mother. When Jesus saw her and the disciple he loved close by, he said to her, "Woman, here is your son!" And to the disciple, he added, "Here is your mother!" From that moment on, the disciple took her into his home.

Darkness fell over the land from noon to three in the afternoon. At that moment, Jesus cried out, "Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani?" which means, "My God, my God, why have you abandoned me?" Some bystanders thought he was calling for Elijah. One man ran and soaked a sponge in vinegar, putting it on a reed to give it to him to drink, but the others said, "Wait, let’s see if Elijah comes to take him down." But Jesus let out a loud cry and breathed his last. At that moment, the Temple curtain tore in two from top to bottom. When the Roman officer in charge saw how he had died, he said, "Surely this man was the Son of God."

From a distance, some women watched, including Mary of Magdala, Mary the mother of James the younger and Joses, and Salome, who had tended to him while he was in Galilee, along with many other women who had come with him to Jerusalem.

As evening approached, just before the Sabbath, Joseph of Arimathea, an honorable member of the Jewish council who was anticipating the Kingdom of God, went to Pilate and bravely asked for Jesus' body. Pilate was surprised to hear he was already dead, so he called the officer to confirm it. After learning that Jesus was indeed dead, he gave the body to Joseph. After taking Jesus down from the cross, Joseph wrapped him in a linen cloth he had bought and placed him in a tomb carved from rock, rolling a stone to seal the tomb's entrance. Mary of Magdala and Mary the mother of Joses watched to see where Jesus was laid.

title: Jesus Conquers Death
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
After the Sabbath came to an end, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome went out to buy spices to prepare Jesus' body. As soon as the sun rose on the first day of the week, they headed to the tomb. They asked each other, "Who will roll the stone away from the entrance for us?" To their surprise, they found that the large stone had already been rolled aside. When they entered the tomb, they saw a young man dressed in a white robe sitting to the right, and they were filled with fear. He spoke to them, saying, "Don’t be afraid. You’re looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has risen; he is not here. Look at the spot where they laid him! Now go and tell his disciples and Peter, 'He is going ahead of you into Galilee; there you will see him, just as he told you.'"

Remembering Jesus' words, they hurried back from the tomb and told the eleven disciples and everyone else what they had seen. However, the story sounded like nonsense to them, and they didn't believe it. Peter, though, jumped up and ran to the tomb. When he looked inside, he found only the linen wrappings; then he went home, puzzled about what had occurred.

Meanwhile, Mary Magdalene was standing outside the tomb, weeping. As she cried, she bent down to look inside and saw two angels dressed in white, one at the head and the other at the feet of where Jesus' body had been. They asked her, "Woman, why are you crying?" She replied, "Because they’ve taken my Master away, and I don’t know where they’ve put him!"

After she said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, though she didn't recognize him. "Woman," he said, "Why are you crying? Who are you looking for?" Thinking he was the gardener, she said, "Sir, if you’ve taken him away, tell me where you’ve laid him, and I will take him away." Jesus called out, "Mary!" She turned to him and exclaimed, "Master!" Jesus replied, "Don’t touch me, for I haven’t ascended to the Father yet. But go to my brothers and tell them, 'I’m ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.'" Mary hurried to the disciples to share her news, saying, "I have seen the Master!" and told them everything he had said.

title: Jesus Walks And Talks With Two Of His Disciples
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
On the same day, two of Jesus' disciples were walking to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem. As they discussed what had happened, Jesus himself approached and walked with them, but they didn't recognize him.

He asked them, "What are you so deeply discussing as you walk along?" They stopped, looking downcast, and one of them, named Cleopas, replied, "Are you just a visitor in Jerusalem? You don't know what has been happening there these past few days?" Jesus asked, "What things?" They explained, "Well, about Jesus of Nazareth, who showed himself to be a prophet, powerful in word and action before God and all the people. Our high priests and rulers handed him over to be condemned to death, and they crucified him. But we were hoping he would be the one to save Israel. Now it’s been three days since all this took place. Some women from our group went to the tomb early this morning and were amazed. They didn’t find his body, and they claimed to have seen a vision of angels who said he’s alive. So some of our people went to the tomb and found it just as the women said, but they didn’t see him."

Then Jesus said to them, "How foolish you are! How slow to believe what the prophets have said! Wasn't it necessary for the Messiah to suffer these things and then enter his glory?"

As they approached the village they were heading to, he acted like he was going to continue on. They urged him, saying, "Stay with us because it's getting late; the day is almost over." So he went in to stay with them.

While they were at the table, he took the bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to them. Then their eyes were opened, and they recognized him, but he vanished from their sight. They said to each other, "Didn’t our hearts burn within us while he spoke to us on the road?"

Immediately, they set out for Jerusalem, where they found the eleven disciples gathered with their friends. They learned that the Lord had truly risen and had appeared to Simon. Then they shared their own experience on the road and how they recognized him when he broke the bread.

title: Jesus Gives His Last Commands To His Helpers
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
While the two disciples were chatting, Jesus suddenly appeared among them. They were scared, thinking they had seen a ghost. But he said to them, "Why are you scared? Why do you doubt? Look at my hands and my feet; it’s really me. Touch me and see for yourselves; a ghost doesn't have flesh and bones like I do." Even though they were filled with joy and disbelief, he asked them, "Do you have anything to eat?" When they handed him a piece of broiled fish, he ate it right in front of them.

Then he said, "Remember what I told you while I was still with you? Everything written about me in the law of Moses, the prophets, and the psalms must come true." He helped them understand the scriptures, saying, "It's written that the Messiah must suffer and rise from the dead on the third day, and that in his name, all nations need to turn away from their sins and receive God's forgiveness. You are to start in Jerusalem and tell everyone about these things."

Now Thomas, one of the twelve disciples, nicknamed "The Twin," wasn’t with them when Jesus arrived. The other disciples told him, "We have seen the Master." But he replied, "I won’t believe it until I see the nail marks in his hands and put my finger where they were, and touch his side."

Eight days later, the disciples were all together again, and Thomas was with them this time. Even though the doors were locked, Jesus came and stood among them, saying, "Peace be with you." Then he said to Thomas, "Put your finger here and look at my hands; reach out and touch my side. Stop doubting and believe." Thomas exclaimed, "My Master and my God!" Jesus replied, "You believe because you’ve seen me? Blessed are those who have not seen and yet still believe!"

Later, Jesus appeared to his disciples by the Sea of Galilee. Simon Peter, Thomas, Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee were together along with two other disciples. Simon Peter said, "I’m going fishing." "We’ll go with you," they replied, and they got into the boat. But that night, they caught nothing. As dawn broke, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples didn’t realize it was him.

title: Jesus Gives His Last Commands To His Helpers
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
He called out to them, "Children, do you have anything to eat?" They answered, "No." He said, "Cast your net on the right side of the boat and you’ll catch something." So they did, and their net was so full of fish they couldn’t haul it in. Then the disciple Jesus loved said to Peter, "It’s the Master!" As soon as Simon Peter heard that, he put on his fishing coat (since he had stripped down for work) and jumped into the water. The other disciples followed in the small boat, dragging the net full of fish.

When they reached the shore, they saw a charcoal fire with fish cooking and bread. Jesus said to them, "Bring some of the fish you just caught." So, Simon Peter went back to the boat and pulled the net ashore, filled with large fish. Even with so many fish, the net didn’t tear. Then Jesus invited them, "Come and have breakfast." None of the disciples dared to ask, "Who are you?" because they knew it was the Master. Jesus served them the bread and fish. This was the third time he showed up to his disciples after he had risen from the dead.

After breakfast, Jesus said to Simon Peter, "Simon, son of John, do you love me more than these?" He replied, "Of course, Master, you know I love you." Jesus said, "Feed my lambs." He asked again, "Simon, son of John, do you love me?" Peter answered, "Yes, Master, you know I love you." Jesus responded, "Tend my sheep." A third time, he asked, "Simon, son of John, do you love me with all your heart?" Peter was hurt that Jesus asked him a third time, "Do you love me?" He replied, "Master, you know everything; you know that I love you." Jesus said, "Feed my sheep."

Then Jesus proclaimed, "All authority has been given to me in heaven and on earth. Therefore, go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit. Teach them to observe everything I have commanded you. And surely, I am with you always, to the very end of the age."

title: Jesus Gives His Last Commands To His Helpers
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Jesus demonstrated, through many proofs, that he was still alive. For forty days, he appeared to his disciples and talked to them about the Kingdom of God. When he was with them, he told them not to leave Jerusalem but to wait for what the Father had promised—the promise he had mentioned before. "John baptized with water, but in just a few days, you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit," he said.

While they were together, they asked him, "Master, is this the time you’re going to restore rulership to Israel?" Jesus replied, "It’s not for you to know the times or seasons that the Father has set by his own authority. But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, throughout Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth." After saying this, he was lifted up, and a cloud took him out of their sight. As they stood gazing into heaven, two men dressed in white appeared beside them, saying, "Men of Galilee, why do you stand looking up into heaven? This Jesus, who has been taken from you into heaven, will come back in the same way you saw him go."

On the Day of Pentecost, while they were all together, a sound like a mighty wind rushed from heaven and filled the entire house where they were sitting. They were filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in languages they hadn’t known, crying out and praising.

When people heard this, a crowd gathered, amazed and confused, asking one another, "What does this mean?" Others sneered, "They are just drunk!"

But Peter, standing with the eleven apostles, spoke to the crowd: "Men of Judea and everyone living in Jerusalem, listen to me! These men aren’t drunk as you think; it’s only nine in the morning. This is what the prophet Joel spoke about:"

"'In the last days,' God declares,
'I will pour out my Spirit on everyone;
Your sons and daughters will prophesy,
Your young men will see visions.
"'And your old men will dream dreams;
Even on my servants, both men and women,
In those days I will pour out my Spirit,
And they will prophesy.'"

"Men of Israel, listen to these words: You crucified and murdered Jesus of Nazareth, a man proved to be from God through miracles, wonders, and signs that God did among you, just as you yourselves know. But God raised him from the dead, freeing him from death because it was impossible for death to keep him. Exalted at God's right hand and having received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit, he poured it out as you now see and hear. Let all of Israel know with certainty that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Messiah."

When the people heard this, they were deeply moved and asked Peter and the rest of the apostles, "Brothers, what should we do?" Peter replied, "Repent and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. Then you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit, which is promised to you, your children, and all who are far off, to everyone whom the Lord our God will call."

With many other words, he urged and pleaded with them to save themselves from this corrupt generation. Those who accepted his message were baptized, and that day about three thousand were added to their number.

title: The Healing Of The Lame Man At The Beautiful Gate
author: The Children's Bible
themes: courage in the face of opposition
text_chunk:
The believers gathered daily at the Temple, sharing meals from one home to another. They enjoyed their food with joy and sincerity, praising God and earning the goodwill of everyone around them. Every day, God brought more people into the community of believers.

One afternoon, Peter and John were on their way to the Temple for prayer. A man, who had been lame since birth, was carried daily to the Beautiful Gate of the Temple to beg from those passing by. When he saw Peter and John approaching, he asked them for help. Peter stared at him, and so did John, saying, "Look at us." The man looked up, hoping they’d give him something. Peter said, "I don’t have any silver or gold, but I can give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk!" He took the man’s right hand and helped him up. Immediately, the man felt strength in his feet and ankles, and he jumped up, stood tall, walked around, and entered the Temple with them, walking, leaping, and praising God.

When the people saw him walking and praising God, they recognized him as the same man who used to sit begging at the Beautiful Gate. They were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened. As he stayed close to Peter and John, the amazed crowd rushed toward them at a place known as Solomon's Porch.

Peter addressed the crowd, saying, "People of Israel, why are you surprised by this? Why are you staring at us as if we made him walk through our own power or good deeds? The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, the God of our ancestors, has glorified Jesus, His servant, whom you betrayed and denied before Pilate when he decided to release him. You chose to deny the Holy and Innocent One and asked for a murderer to be released, killing the One who gives life! But God raised Him from the dead, and we are witnesses to that. It is faith in His name that has healed this man in front of you."

"Brothers, I know you acted out of ignorance, just like your leaders. Turn back to God and live rightly so that your sins may be forgiven, and God will send you strength. After raising His servant from the dead, God sent Him first to you to bless you by turning you away from your wrongdoing."

title: The Healing Of The Lame Man At The Beautiful Gate
author: The Children's Bible
themes: courage in the face of opposition
text_chunk:
While they were speaking, they were interrupted by the priests, the Temple officer, and the Sadducees. They were upset because Peter and John were teaching the people and sharing the news about Jesus rising from the dead. They arrested the apostles, and since it was already evening, they put them in jail until the next day.

The next morning, a meeting took place in Jerusalem with the rulers, elders, and scribes. The high priest Annas, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and all the high priest's family were present. They brought the apostles before them and asked, "By what power or name did you do this?"

Filled with the Holy Spirit, Peter replied, "Rulers and elders of Israel, if we are being questioned today about a good deed done to a lame man, let it be known to all of you and to the people of Israel that this man is standing before you healed through the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom you crucified and whom God raised from the dead. He is the stone you builders rejected, which has now become the cornerstone. Salvation is found in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given to humanity by which we must be saved."

When the rulers saw how bold Peter and John were, and realized they were uneducated and ordinary men, they were amazed. They remembered that these men had been with Jesus. After sending them out of the council, they said to each other, "What are we going to do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about the miraculous sign they performed, and we can’t deny it. But to prevent it from spreading further, let’s warn them not to speak anymore in the name of Jesus." So they called them back in and ordered them not to teach or speak at all in the name of Jesus. Peter and John replied, "You decide whether it’s right in God’s eyes to listen to you rather than to Him; we can’t help speaking about what we have seen and heard."

Seeing the healed man standing next to them, the rulers had nothing to say. After issuing more threats, they let the apostles go, unable to find a reason to punish them because all the people were praising God for what had happened.

title: The Generosity Of The Early Christians
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
All the believers were united in heart and mind, and they didn’t think of their possessions as their own. Instead, they shared everything with one another. Everyone's needs were met, because those who owned land or homes sold them and brought the money to the apostles. This money was distributed to anyone who needed it. Joseph, known as Barnabas by the apostles, sold his farm and gave the proceeds to them.

However, a man named Ananias and his wife Sapphira sold a piece of property. With her agreement, they kept back a portion of the money and only brought part of it to the apostles. "Ananias," Peter said, "why has Satan filled your heart with the urge to lie to the Holy Spirit and withhold part of the money from the sale? While the land was yours, didn’t you have control over it? And after selling it, wasn’t the money yours to handle as you wished? How could you even think of doing this? You haven’t lied just to people, but to God." When Ananias heard this, he fell down and died; a great fear gripped everyone who heard about it. The younger men stood up, wrapped up his body, and took it away for burial.

About three hours later, his wife came in, completely unaware of what had happened. Peter asked her, "Did you sell the land for this amount?" She replied, "Yes, for that amount." Then Peter said, "How could you two conspire to test the Spirit of the Lord? The men who buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out too." Instantly, she fell dead at his feet; when the young men came in and found her dead, they took her away and buried her next to her husband.

A deep fear then spread throughout the entire church and among everyone who heard about these events. Many wonders and miracles were performed by the apostles among the people. Even crowds from nearby towns flocked in, bringing sick individuals, and everyone was healed.

title: Stephen The First Christian Martyr
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
In those days, as the number of disciples was growing quickly, the Greek-speaking Jews started to complain about those from Palestine, saying their widows were being overlooked during the daily food distribution. So the twelve apostles gathered all the disciples and said, "We can’t stop preaching to serve tables. Choose seven men from among you who have a good reputation, are wise, and are spiritually strong—we'll assign them to this task. Meanwhile, we'll continue focusing on prayer and sharing the good news." Everyone agreed, and they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and the Holy Spirit, along with Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus, who was from Antioch but had become a Jew. They brought these men before the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.

The message of the Lord kept spreading, and the number of disciples in Jerusalem grew significantly. Even many priests accepted the faith.

Stephen, who had a compelling presence and great power, performed amazing wonders and miracles among the people. Some from the Synagogue of the Libyans and Jews from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia began to debate with Stephen, but they couldn't overcome him due to the wisdom and spirit with which he spoke.

So they secretly bribed some people to say, "We heard him speaking blasphemous words against Moses and God." This stirred up the crowd, the elders, and the scribes, leading them to seize Stephen and bring him before the council. They also hired false witnesses who claimed, "This man speaks against this holy place and the law. We heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs handed down by Moses!"

All those sitting in the council fixed their eyes on Stephen, seeing his face shining like that of an angel. The high priest then asked, "Are these charges true?" Stephen replied, "Brothers and fathers, listen. The Most High God doesn’t dwell in buildings made by humans.

"You stiff-necked and stubborn people! You always resist the Holy Spirit, just like your ancestors did. Which of the prophets didn’t your forefathers persecute? They even killed those who predicted the coming of the Righteous One, whom you have betrayed and murdered—you who received the law that was delivered by angels but haven’t kept it!"

When they heard these words, they were enraged and ground their teeth at him. But Stephen, filled with the Holy Spirit, gazed into heaven and saw the glory of God and Jesus standing at the right hand of God. "Look! I see heaven opened," he exclaimed, "and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God!" They covered their ears and yelled, rushing at him all at once. They threw him out of the city and stoned him. The men who threw the first stones laid their cloaks at the feet of a young man named Saul. While they stoned Stephen, he prayed, "Lord Jesus, receive my spirit!" Then he knelt down and shouted, "Lord, don’t hold this sin against them!" After saying this, he fell asleep. Some devout men buried Stephen, mourning deeply for him.

Meanwhile, Saul approved of the murder. He also sought to destroy the church, going from house to house, dragging men and women off to prison.

title: Philip And The Ethiopian
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
After Stephen was stoned to death, a huge wave of persecution hit the church in Jerusalem, causing everyone except the apostles to scatter across Judea and Samaria. Those who fled spread the good news about Jesus wherever they went.

Philip traveled to the city of Samaria, where he began sharing the message of Jesus, the Christ. The crowds paid close attention to him as they witnessed the miracles he performed. Many who couldn’t walk or were lame found healing, filling the city with joy. Both men and women who believed Philip’s message about the Kingdom of God and the name of Jesus were baptized.

When the apostles in Jerusalem heard that the Samaritans had accepted God's message, they sent Peter and John to visit them. Upon their arrival, they prayed that the Samaritans would receive the Holy Spirit, which had not yet been given to anyone; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus. After this, Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.

After preaching the word of the Lord, the apostles returned to Jerusalem, sharing the good news in many Samaritan villages along the way. But then an angel of the Lord spoke to Philip, saying, "Get up and head south on the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza." On his journey, he encountered an Ethiopian official who was in charge of the treasures of Candace, the queen of the Ethiopians. He had traveled to Jerusalem to worship and was now on his way home. As the Ethiopian rode in his chariot reading the prophet Isaiah, the Spirit told Philip, "Go over and talk to that man in the chariot." Philip ran up and heard him reading from Isaiah, so he asked, "Do you understand what you’re reading?" The Ethiopian replied, "How can I unless someone explains it to me?" He then invited Philip to climb up and sit with him.

The scripture he was reading went like this:

"He was led like a sheep to the slaughter,
And like a lamb that is silent before its shearer,
So he did not open his mouth."

The Ethiopian turned to Philip and said, "Please, can you tell me who the prophet is speaking about? Is it himself or someone else?" Philip took this opportunity to explain and shared the good news about Jesus. As they traveled along, they came across some water, and the Ethiopian exclaimed, "Look, here’s water! What’s stopping me from being baptized?" He commanded the chariot to stop, and both of them went down into the water, where Philip baptized him. When they emerged from the water, the Spirit of the Lord suddenly took Philip away, and the Ethiopian didn't see him again. Filled with joy, he continued on his way, while Philip preached the good news in every town until he reached Cæsarea.

title: Peter And The Roman Officer
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
While Peter traveled throughout the region, he spent a significant amount of time in Joppa, staying at the home of Simon, a tanner.

Cornelius, the leader of the Italian guard, lived in Cæsarea. He was a good man, and he and his whole family worshipped God. He generously shared with the poor and prayed to God regularly.

One day, around three in the afternoon, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel of God approach him and call out, "Cornelius." Even though he was scared, Cornelius replied, "What is it, Lord?" The angel said, "Your prayers and your gifts to the poor have made a strong impression on God. Now send some men to Joppa to bring back a certain Simon, known as Peter. He’s staying with Simon, a tanner, whose house is right by the sea." After the angel left, Cornelius called two of his servants and a trusted soldier who always waited on him. He explained everything to them before sending them to Joppa.

The next day, around noon, as they approached Joppa, Peter went up onto the rooftop to pray. While he was praying, the men sent by Cornelius found Simon's house, stood by the gate, and called out to ask if Simon, also known as Peter, was staying there. The Spirit told Peter, "Look, three men are looking for you. Go down and don’t hesitate to go with them, because I have sent them." Peter went down to the men and said, "I’m the one you’re looking for. What brings you here?" They replied, "Cornelius, a captain of the guard, a righteous man who worships God and is well spoken of by all the Jews, was instructed by a holy angel to summon you to his house so he can hear what you have to say." Peter welcomed them in, and they stayed the night.

title: Peter And The Roman Officer
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
The following day, Peter got up and went with them, and some of the disciples from Joppa went along as well. The day after that, they arrived in Cæsarea. Cornelius was expecting them and had gathered his relatives and close friends. When Peter entered, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet to worship him. But Peter said, "Get up; I’m just a man like you." After talking with him, Peter went inside and found a large group of people assembled. Cornelius explained, "Just four days ago, I was praying in my house around this time, and a man in shining clothes stood before me and said, 'Cornelius, your prayer has been heard, and your gifts to the poor have been noticed by God. Send someone to Joppa to find Simon, who is also called Peter; he’s staying with Simon, a tanner, by the sea.' So I sent for you right away, and I’m so glad you came. We’re all here in the presence of God, eager to hear what the Lord has commanded you to share."

Peter began, "I truly understand that God shows no favoritism; in every nation, anyone who honors Him and does what is right is accepted by Him."

While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell on everyone who was listening. The Jewish disciples who had come with Peter were amazed that the gift of the Holy Spirit was also given to those who weren’t Jews. They heard them speaking in tongues and praising God. Then Peter asked, "Can anyone refuse to baptize these people who have received the Holy Spirit just as we have?" He ordered that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. They then requested him to stay with them for several days.

When Peter returned to Jerusalem, the Jewish believers criticized him for associating with those who weren’t Jews. But Peter explained, "I was in Joppa praying when three men stood outside the house where we were, sent from Cæsarea. The Spirit told me to go with them, even though they weren’t Jews. Six of my brothers here went with me, and we entered the man’s home. He told us how he had seen the angel standing in his house, saying, 'Send to Joppa and bring Simon, also called Peter. He will tell you how you and your entire household can be saved.' As I began to preach, the Holy Spirit fell on them just like it did on us at the beginning. I remembered the Lord’s words, 'John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.' So, if God gave them the same gift He gave us when we believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, who was I to argue with God?" When they heard this, they stopped criticizing him and praised God, saying, "So God has also granted repentance to those who aren’t Jews, allowing them to live."

title: Peter'S Escape From Prison
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk: At that time, King Herod started targeting the members of the church, and he killed James, the brother of John, with a sword. When Herod saw that this pleased the Jewish leaders, he also seized Peter. This was during the Feast of Unleavened Bread. After arresting Peter, he put him in prison, assigning four squads of four soldiers each to guard him. Herod planned to present Peter to the people after Passover.

title: Peter'S Escape From Prison
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk: So Peter was locked up in prison, but the church members prayed fervently to God for him. On the night before Herod intended to bring him out for trial, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, chained to them. Guards were stationed at the doors as well. Suddenly, an angel of the Lord appeared, and a bright light illuminated the cell. The angel struck Peter on the side, waking him up, and said, "Get up quickly." Instantly, the chains fell off his hands. The angel instructed him, "Put on your belt and sandals." Peter did as he was told. The angel then said, "Wrap your cloak around you and follow me." So Peter followed him out, not realizing that what the angel was doing was real; he thought he was dreaming. They passed by the first and second guards and reached the iron gate leading to the city, which opened by itself. They stepped out and walked down one street, and then, just like that, the angel left him. Once alone, Peter said, "Now I truly know that the Lord has sent his angel and rescued me from Herod’s grasp and from what the Jews expected to do to me." After considering his next steps, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John Mark, where many people had gathered to pray. When he knocked at the door, a girl named Rhoda came to answer. She was so thrilled to hear Peter’s voice that she forgot to open the door and ran to tell everyone that Peter was outside. They replied, "You're out of your mind." But she insisted it was true. They said, "It must be his angel." Meanwhile, Peter kept knocking, and when they finally opened the door and saw him, they were astonished. He waved his hand for them to be quiet and explained how the Lord had freed him from prison. Then he said, "Go tell James and the other brothers." After that, he left and went to another location. The next morning, there was a lot of commotion among the soldiers about what had happened to Peter. After searching for him and not finding him, Herod questioned the guards and ordered that they should be executed.

title: Paul'S Account Of What He Saw And Heard On His Way To Damascus
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
I’m a Jew, born in Tarsus, Cilicia, but I grew up in Jerusalem. I was educated as a disciple of Gamaliel, following the strict traditions of our ancestors, and I was truly devoted to God, just like all of you.

You know what I did in the past among the Jewish people. I severely persecuted the church of God, trying to wipe it out completely. Driven by my strong commitment to the laws and customs passed down from my forefathers, I went further than anyone else from my hometown. I genuinely believed it was my responsibility to fight against the movement of Jesus of Nazareth. I did this in Jerusalem, where I had authority from the high priests to imprison many of Jesus' followers. When they were executed, I voted for it. I frequently punished them in synagogues, trying to force them to denounce the name of Jesus. In my uncontrolled rage, I even pursued them to far-off cities.

On my way to Damascus for this very purpose, with written permission from the high priests, I encountered a blinding light from heaven around noon, brighter than the sun itself, shining on me and my traveling companions. We all fell to the ground, and I heard a voice speaking to me in Hebrew, "Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me? It’s tough for you to kick against the goads." I replied, "Who are you, Lord?" and the Lord said, "I am Jesus, the one you are persecuting. Get up and stand on your feet, for I have appeared to you to appoint you as my servant and a witness for what you have seen and for what I will show you. I chose you from the Jews and other nations to open their eyes, turning them from darkness to light, from the power of Satan to God, so they may receive forgiveness for their sins and become part of those who believe in me."

When God, who set me apart even before I was born and called me with his love, decided to reveal His Son in me so that I could share the good news with all nations, I immediately went into Arabia instead of talking to anyone else or going straight to Jerusalem to meet the apostles who were there before me. Upon my return, I preached first in Damascus. The governor appointed by King Aretas sent guards to the city to capture me, but I managed to escape by being lowered in a basket through a window in the city wall.

After three years, I went to Jerusalem to see Peter and stayed with him for fifteen days. I didn’t meet anyone else among the apostles except for James, the brother of Jesus.

title: The Christian Church At Antioch
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
After the persecution that followed Stephen's death, the disciples scattered to places like Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they only shared the message of Jesus with fellow Jews. However, some of them, who hailed from Cyprus and Cyrene, also began to share the good news about the Lord Jesus with the Greeks when they arrived in Antioch. The Lord's power was with them, leading many to believe and turn to Him.

When the news of this reached the church in Jerusalem, they sent Barnabas to Antioch. Once he witnessed God's grace at work, he was filled with joy and encouraged everyone to stay faithful to the Lord; as a result, many more people came to believe.

Barnabas then traveled to Tarsus to find Paul, who was previously known as Saul. After locating him, he brought Paul back to Antioch, where they spent a full year teaching a large number of people in the church. It was in Antioch that the disciples were first called Christians.

During this period, some prophets arrived in Antioch from Jerusalem. One of them was Agabus, who, inspired by the Spirit, announced that a severe famine would strike the region, which indeed occurred during the reign of Emperor Claudius. As a response, the disciples, each according to their means, sent aid to their brothers living in Judea. They entrusted their gifts to Barnabas and Paul. After fulfilling their mission, Barnabas and Paul returned from Jerusalem, bringing back with them John, also known as Mark.

In the church at Antioch, there were prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Symeon (nicknamed Niger), Lucius from Cyrene, a childhood friend of Herod the ruler named Manean, and Paul. While they were worshiping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit spoke, saying, "Set apart for me Barnabas and Paul for the work I have called them to do." After they had fasted and prayed, they blessed them and sent them on their way.

title: Paul And Barnabas In Foreign Lands
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Paul and Barnabas, guided by the Holy Spirit, traveled to Seleucia and then sailed to Cyprus. When they arrived in Salamis, accompanied by Mark, they shared God’s message in the Jewish synagogue.

After exploring the entire island up to Paphos, they set sail again, and Paul and his companions arrived in Perga in Pamphylia. There, Mark decided to leave them and return to Jerusalem, while Paul and Barnabas continued on to Antioch. On the Sabbath, they went into the synagogue and took their seats. After the law and the prophets were read, the leaders of the synagogue sent a message to them, saying, "Brothers, if you have any encouraging words for the people, please share them." Paul stood up, motioned with his hand, and said, "Listen, fellow Israelites and you who worship God. The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors. When they were in Egypt, He made them a great nation, and with powerful signs, He led them out of that land. After defeating seven nations in Canaan, He gave their land to them and later appointed David to be their king. From David's lineage, God has brought to Israel, as He promised, a Savior, Jesus."

"Brothers, descendants of Abraham and all who fear God, this saving message has been sent to us. The people in Jerusalem and their leaders didn’t believe in Jesus, and even though they couldn’t find any reason to execute Him, they urged Pilate to have Him killed. But God raised Him from the dead, and for many days, He appeared to those who had come with Him from Galilee to Jerusalem. They are now His witnesses to the people. So we bring you the good news that God, by raising Jesus, has fulfilled the promise made to our ancestors for our children."

As Paul and Barnabas were leaving, the people pleaded for them to repeat their message the following Sabbath. Once the congregation dispersed, many Jews and religious Greeks followed Paul and Barnabas, who encouraged them to stay strong with God’s help.

title: Paul And Barnabas In Foreign Lands
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
By the next Sabbath, nearly everyone in the city came to hear the Lord's message. However, when the Jews saw the large crowd, they became envious and started to contradict Paul, insulting him. But Paul and Barnabas stood their ground, declaring, "We had to speak God’s message to you first; but since you refuse to listen and show that you aren’t worthy of eternal life, we now turn to those who aren’t Jews. This is the Lord’s command to us: ‘I have made you a light for the nations, so that you may bring salvation to the ends of the earth.’"

When the non-Jews heard this, they were thrilled and praised God's message; and everyone who was chosen for eternal life believed. As God's message spread throughout the region, the Jews, with support from high-ranking women and influential men in the city, stirred up persecution against Paul and Barnabas, driving them out of the city. The apostles shook the dust off their feet in protest and moved on to Iconium. Meanwhile, the new disciples were filled with joy and the Holy Spirit.

In Iconium, Paul and Barnabas entered the Jewish synagogue and spoke so effectively that many Jews and Greeks came to believe. But the unbelieving Jews stirred up opposition among the other groups and poisoned their minds against the apostles. The townspeople were divided, some siding with the Jews and others with the apostles. A plot was made by both the other groups and the Jews, along with their leaders, to attack and stone the apostles; however, they found out and escaped to the towns of Lystra and Derbe, where they continued to share the good news.

In Lystra, there was a man who couldn’t use his feet; he had been lame since birth and had never walked. While listening to Paul preach, the apostle locked eyes with him and, seeing that he had the faith to be healed, shouted, "Stand up on your feet!" The man jumped up and began to walk. When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they shouted in their own language, "The gods have come down to us in human form!" They called Barnabas "Zeus" and Paul "Hermes," since he was the main speaker. The priests of the temple of Zeus, which was in front of the town, brought oxen and wreaths to the entrance, eager to join the crowd in offering sacrifices to them.

But when Barnabas and Paul heard what was happening, they tore their clothes and rushed into the crowd, shouting, "Men, why are you doing this?
