We are just men like you, bringing you the good news so that you can turn from these idols and worship the living God, who made the heavens, the earth, the sea, and everything in them. In times past, He allowed all nations to go their own way; yet He did not leave Himself without a witness, for He provided rain from heaven and fruitful seasons, filling your hearts with food and joy." Even after this, they could barely stop the crowd from sacrificing to them.

However, Jews came from Antioch and Iconium and persuaded the crowd, who then stoned Paul and left him for dead outside the city. But when the disciples gathered around him, he got up and went back into the city.

The next day, he traveled with Barnabas to Derbe. After preaching the good news in that city and winning over many disciples, they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch to encourage the disciples, urging them to stay faithful. They then traveled through Pisidia and arrived in Pamphylia. After preaching in Perga, they made their way down to Attaleia. Finally, they set sail back to Antioch.

Upon arriving in Antioch, they gathered the church members and shared everything God had done through them, including how He had opened the door of faith to the non-Jews. They stayed there for a significant amount of time with the disciples.

title: Why Paul Went To Macedonia
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
After spending some time in Antioch, Paul turned to Barnabas and said, "Let’s head back and visit the brothers in all the towns where we shared the good news from the Lord, and see how they’re doing." Barnabas wanted to bring Mark along, but Paul felt it wasn't a good idea to include someone who had left them in Pamphylia instead of continuing on with them in Pisidia. So, they decided to part ways—Barnabas took Mark with him to Cyprus, while Paul chose Silas and headed off, supported by the brothers who commended him to the Lord’s care. He traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.

He also made his way to Derbe and Lystra. In Lystra, there was a disciple named Timothy, who was the son of a Christian Jewish mother and a Greek father. Because Timothy had a great reputation among the believers in Lystra and Iconium, Paul wanted him to join their journey. The churches grew stronger in faith and saw an increase in numbers every day.

Paul and his companions crossed through the regions of Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit kept them from sharing the message in Asia. When they arrived in Mysia, they tried to enter Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus wouldn’t allow it. So, they passed through Mysia and went down to Troas.

One night, Paul had a vision: a man from Macedonia was standing there, begging him, "Come to Macedonia and help us." As soon as Paul saw the vision, we were eager to head straight to Macedonia, believing that God had called us to share the good news there. Setting sail from Troas, we sailed directly to Samothrace, and the following day, we arrived in Neapolis. From there, we traveled to Philippi, the most important city in that region of Macedonia, where we spent several days.

On the Sabbath, we went outside the city gate by the river, thinking we would find a place for prayer. We sat down and began talking to the women who had gathered there. Among them was Lydia, a dealer in purple cloth from Thyatira, who was already a worshipper of God. The Lord opened her heart to pay attention to what Paul was saying; after she and her household were baptized, she urged us, saying, "If you’re convinced that I am a true believer in the Lord, please come and stay at my house." And she insisted that we do.

title: Paul And Silas In Macedonia
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
One day, as we were heading to the prayer meeting, a slave girl approached us. She was under the control of a spirit that gave her the ability to predict the future, which made her owners a lot of money through fortune-telling. She kept following Paul and the rest of us, shouting, "These men are servants of the Most High God; they’re telling you how to be saved!" She did this for many days until Paul, unable to take it anymore, turned and said to the spirit, "In the name of Jesus Christ, I command you to come out of her." And just like that, it left her.

When her owners realized their chance to make money was gone, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into the public square to face the city officials. They brought them before the military leaders, saying, "These Jews are causing trouble in our city; they’re promoting customs that aren’t lawful for us Romans to adopt or follow." The crowd quickly joined in the attack, and the military rulers ripped off their clothes and ordered them to be beaten with rods. After giving them a severe beating, they threw them into prison and told the jailer to keep them secure. Following this order, the jailer locked them in the inner prison and secured their feet in stocks.

Around midnight, while Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, the other prisoners were listening. Suddenly, there was a massive earthquake that shook the very foundations of the prison. All the doors flew open, and the chains that bound all the prisoners fell away.

When the jailer woke up and saw the prison doors wide open, he drew his sword, ready to take his own life, thinking the prisoners had escaped. But Paul shouted, "Don’t harm yourself; we’re all here!" The jailer called for lights, rushed in, and trembling with fear, fell down before Paul and Silas. He brought them out and said, "Sirs, what must I do to be saved?" They replied, "Believe in the Lord Jesus Christ, and you will be saved—you and your household." Then Paul and Silas shared the word of the Lord with him and his entire family. At that hour, the jailer cleaned and bandaged their wounds, and he and his family were baptized right then. He took them to his house, served them a meal, and celebrated with his family for believing in God.

title: Paul And Silas In Macedonia
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
The next morning, the city officials sent the police with orders, "Release these men." The jailer told Paul, "The police have come with an order for your release; you can go now and be at peace." But Paul replied, "They beat us publicly without a trial, even though we are Roman citizens, and threw us into prison! Now they want to send us out secretly? No way! Let them come here and escort us out themselves."

The police reported this to the military leaders, who, when they heard Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, became frightened. They came to make amends, escorted them out of prison, and pleaded with them to leave the city. So, Paul and Silas left the prison and went to Lydia's house; after meeting with the believers and encouraging them, they departed from the city.

After traveling through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they arrived in Thessalonica, where there was a synagogue of the Jews. As usual, Paul went in and engaged them in discussion for three weeks, using the scriptures to show that the Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead, declaring, 'This Jesus I’m talking about is the Christ.' Some of the Jews and a significant number of God-fearing Greeks and leading women accepted his message and joined Paul and Silas.

However, the Jews became jealous, rallied some of the troublemakers from the marketplace, and incited a riot against the city. They went to Jason's house to find Paul and Silas, but when they didn’t find them, they dragged Jason and some other believers before the city officials, shouting, "These men who have been turning the world upside down have come here too! Jason has welcomed them, and they don’t obey the laws of Caesar. They claim that someone named Jesus is king!" The crowd and the officials were troubled by this, but after Jason and the others promised to maintain peace, they let them go.

Immediately, the brothers sent Paul and Silas away by night to Berœa. Upon their arrival, they went to the Jewish synagogue, where the people were open-minded and ready to accept the teachings about Jesus. They even examined their scriptures daily to verify what Paul was saying. Many Jews believed, along with prominent Greek women and several men.

As soon as the Jews in Thessalonica heard that God’s message was being spread by Paul in Berœa, they came there too to stir up trouble. So the brothers quickly sent Paul away to the coastline, while Silas and Timothy stayed in Berœa. The friends who accompanied Paul went as far as Athens, where they left him, after receiving instructions to tell Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as they could.

title: Paul'S Great Speech At Athens
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
While Paul was waiting in Athens for Silas and Timothy, he felt a surge of anger when he noticed that the city was filled with idols. He started debating in the synagogue with the Jews and the Greeks who worshipped with them, as well as every day with anyone he met in the marketplace. A few philosophers encountered him too. Some of them remarked, "What does this collector of random knowledge have to say?" Others said, "He seems to be promoting some new gods." This was because he had been sharing the good news about Jesus and how He had risen from the dead. They brought him to the Court of Areopagus and asked, "Can we hear about this new teaching of yours? What you’re saying sounds strange to us, so we want to understand it better." (All the Athenians and the visiting foreigners spent their time either talking about or listening to something new.)

So, Paul stood in the center of the Court and said, "People of Athens, I notice that you are very religious in every way, for as I walked around and observed your objects of worship, I found an altar that had the inscription,

‘To an Unknown God.’ Therefore, the God you are worshipping without knowing, I want to tell you about. This God who created the world and everything in it is the Lord of heaven and earth and doesn’t live in temples built by human hands. He isn't served by our hands as though He needed anything, for it's He who gives life and breath and everything to all of us. He made every nation from one bloodline so that they would inhabit the whole earth. He has set boundaries for them—when and where they should live—so they would seek God, perhaps feeling their way toward Him and finding Him, because He is not far from any of us. In Him, we live and move and exist, as some of your own poets have said, 'We are also His children.'

"So, as children of God, we shouldn’t think of the divine as something made of gold, silver, or stone, formed by human creativity. God overlooked the times of ignorance, but now He commands everyone everywhere to repent, for He has set a day to judge the world fairly through the person He has chosen; He has given proof of this to everyone by raising Him from the dead."

When they heard about the resurrection, some mocked him, while others said, "We want to hear more about this from you another time." So Paul left them. However, some men joined him and believed, including Dionysius, a member of the Court of the Areopagus, a woman named Damaris, and several others. After this, Paul departed Athens and headed to Corinth.

title: Paul Writes To His Friends At Thessalonica
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Paul, Silas, and Timothy are sending greetings to the church in Thessalonica, which is rooted in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

May you be filled with goodwill and peace.

We always thank God for all of you and keep you in our prayers. We constantly remember your active faith, loving service, and strong hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.

You know, dear friends, that our visit with you had a significant impact. Remember how we had the courage, with God's help, to share the good news at Philippi, even after being mistreated? We cared for you deeply, so much so that we would have gladly given you not just the message of God but our very lives.

You also remember how hard we worked, day and night, so we wouldn't be a burden to you while sharing God's good news. You are witnesses to our sincere, fair, and respectful approach. We treated each of you just like a father would treat his children, encouraging you and urging you to live in a way that honors the God who calls you into His Kingdom and glory.

We constantly thank God for this too: when you received God’s message from us, you accepted it not just as human words, but as it truly is—the word of God, which is actively working in the hearts of those of you who believe. You’ve followed the example of the churches in Judea, which are united with Jesus Christ, because you’ve faced the same struggles from your fellow countrymen as they did from the Jews who crucified the Lord Jesus.

Brothers, even when we were separated from you for a short time (out of sight, but never out of mind), we were eager to see you face to face. I wanted to visit you—Paul wanted to many times—but obstacles were in our way. Who is "our hope, our joy, our crown" that we have every right to boast about? Isn't it you? Because you are our pride and joy!

So, when I couldn’t hold back any longer, I felt it was best to stay alone in Athens and send Timothy, our brother and God’s servant who spreads the good news about Christ, to strengthen your faith. We wanted to encourage you so that none of you would be shaken by the troubles you're facing, because you know they’re a part of life.

But now that Timothy has returned with wonderful news about your faith and love, and how you always remember me fondly and look forward to seeing me like I do with you, I’ve found great comfort, dear friends, in all my struggles because of your faith.

How can we ever thank God enough for the joy you bring us? Day and night, we earnestly pray to see your faces and help strengthen your faith. May our God and Father and our Lord Jesus guide our way to you, and may the Lord make your love for one another and for everyone grow stronger, just as our love for you does, so your hearts will be strong and your character blameless in the eyes of our God and Father.

I urge you in the name of the Lord to have this letter read aloud to everyone. May the love of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.

title: Paul'S Work At Corinth
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Paul left that place and went to the home of Titius Justus, a man who worshiped God. His house was right next to the synagogue. Crispus, the head of the synagogue, along with his whole family, believed in the Lord, and many Corinthians who listened to Paul also believed and got baptized. One night, the Lord spoke to Paul in a vision, saying, "Don't be afraid. Speak out without holding back, because I am with you, and no one will harm you; I have many followers in this city." So, Paul stayed there for a year and a half, teaching them God’s word.

When Gallio became the governor of Greece, the Jews came together to attack Paul, bringing him to court with the allegation that he was leading people to worship God in ways that went against the law. Just as Paul was about to defend himself, Gallio interrupted and said to the Jews, "If this were a matter of wrongdoing or serious crime, I would have a reason to listen to you, Jews. But if your issues are just about names and your own laws, you handle them yourselves. I refuse to judge disputes like these." With that, he dismissed them from the court. The crowd then grabbed Sosthenes, the head of the synagogue, and beat him right there; but Gallio didn't seem to care. After spending some more time in Corinth, Paul said goodbye to his fellow believers and, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila, set sail for Syria.

title: Paul Writes To His Friends In Corinth
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Paul, chosen to be an apostle of Christ Jesus by God's will, and our brother Sosthenes, send greetings to the church of God in Corinth.

I constantly thank God for the incredible blessings He has given you through Jesus Christ. Because of Him, you've received every kind of speech and knowledge, proving the truth of the testimony I shared about Christ when I was with you.

Brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, I urge you all to unite in your beliefs. There should be no divisions among you; instead, aim to be of one mind and purpose. I've heard from Chloe's family that there are arguments among you.

Steer clear of all impurity! Every other sin a person commits happens outside the body, but an impure person sins against their own body. Don't you know that your body is the temple of the Holy Spirit within you, which you have received from God? You don’t belong to yourselves; you were bought at a price. Honor God with your bodies.

No temptation has come your way that you can’t resist. God is faithful and won’t allow you to be tempted beyond your limits; when temptation comes, He will provide a way out so you can endure it.

I have the freedom to do anything, but not everything is beneficial for me. I can do as I please, but not everything makes me a better person. Each of us should look out not only for our own interests but also for the interests of others.

Don’t you realize that in a race, even though everyone runs, only one person wins the prize? Run in such a way that you may win. Every athlete practices self-control; they work for a crown that fades away, but we strive for one that lasts forever. So I run with purpose, not like a boxer who merely shadows punches; I train my body and control it, lest I, having preached to others, become disqualified.

Now, brothers, I want you to understand spiritual gifts. There are different types of gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit. There are different ways of serving, but all serve the same Master. God's power shows in various ways, but it is the same God at work in all of you. Each one is given a unique gift of the Spirit for the common good.

title: Paul Writes To His Friends In Corinth
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Just like a body is made up of many parts that function together as one, so it is with Christ. We have all been baptized by one Spirit to form one body. Whether we are Jews or Greeks, slaves or free, we've all received the same Spirit. The body comprises many parts, not just one. If a foot says, 'I’m not a hand, so I don’t belong to the body,' that doesn’t make it any less a part of the body. And if an ear says, 'I’m not an eye, so I don’t belong to the body,' it’s still part of the body. If the whole body were an eye, how would we hear? If the whole body were an ear, how would we smell? But God arranged the parts of the body as He wanted. If there were only one part, where would the body be? There are many parts, but one body.

The eye can’t say to the hand, 'I don’t need you,' nor can the head say to the feet, 'I don’t need you.' In fact, the parts that seem weaker are essential. If one part suffers, every part suffers with it; if one part is honored, every part rejoices.

Now you are the body of Christ, and each one of you is a part of it. God arranged the body as He saw fit, putting apostles first, then prophets, teachers, miracle workers, healers, helpers, and leaders.

Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Can everyone work miracles? Are all healers? Can everyone interpret words? Always strive for the highest gifts.

Yet, I will show you a much better way. If I speak in the languages of men and angels but don’t have love, I’m just a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal. Even if I have the gift of prophecy and understand all mysteries and knowledge, and have enough faith to move mountains, without love, I am nothing. If I give away everything I have to the poor and even sacrifice my body, but don’t have love, I gain nothing.

Love is patient and kind; it doesn’t envy or boast, it’s not proud. It doesn’t dishonor others, it’s not self-seeking, it’s not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs. Love doesn’t delight in evil but rejoices in the truth. Love bears all things, believes all things, hopes for all things, endures all things.

Love never fails. Prophecies will fade away, tongues will cease, and knowledge will come to an end; because we know only in part, and we prophesy only in part. But when the perfect comes, the imperfect will end.

When I was a child, I spoke, thought, and reasoned like a child; but when I became an adult, I put away childish things. Now we see a dim reflection in a mirror, but then we will see face to face. Now I know in part, but then I will know fully, just as I am fully known.

And now these three remain: faith, hope, and love. But the greatest of these is love.

title: The New Life After Death
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Hey, everyone, remember the great news I shared with you? Christ died for our sins, was buried, and then rose again on the third day.

If we're saying that Christ came back to life, why do some of you claim there’s no resurrection for the dead? If there’s no resurrection, that means Christ didn’t rise either; and if Christ didn’t rise, then our message is pointless, and your faith is pointless too.

But someone might ask, "How do the dead come back to life, and what kind of bodies will they have?" That’s a silly question! Think about a seed: it doesn’t sprout unless it first dies. What you plant isn’t the body that’s going to be, but just a seed, like a grain of wheat. God gives it the sort of body He sees fit; each seed gets its own unique body.

Not all flesh is the same: there’s human flesh, the flesh of animals, the flesh of birds, and the flesh of fish. There are heavenly bodies as well as earthly ones, but the beauty of the heavenly bodies is different from that of the earthly ones. The sun has one kind of brightness, the moon has another, and each star shines in its own unique way.

It’s the same with a person’s body when they rise from the dead. It’s sown as something that can decay, but it’s raised as something that can’t decay. It’s sown without honor, but it’s raised in glory; it’s sown in weakness, but it’s raised in power; it’s sown as a natural body, but it’s raised as a spiritual body. If there’s a natural body, there’s also a spiritual body.

In an instant, in the blink of an eye, we’ll be changed. This perishable body will put on something that lasts forever, and this mortal body will become immortal. Then what’s written in Scripture will come to pass: "Death has been swallowed up by victory. O grave, where is your victory? O death, where is your sting?" Thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.

title: Ways Of Showing Love
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Now, about the collection for God's people in Jerusalem: you should follow the same instructions I gave to the churches in Galatia. Every first day of the week, each of you should set aside a portion of what you've earned, so that when I arrive, there won't need to be a rush to collect it. When I get there, I’ll send the people you choose, along with letters, to deliver your gift to Jerusalem. If it seems right for me to go with them, they can accompany me.

I plan to visit you after I pass through Macedonia since I'm heading there. I might spend some time with you, even the winter, so that you can help me on my journey, wherever that takes me. I don’t want to just pop in for a quick visit; my hope is to stay awhile with you if the Lord allows. But I will be in Ephesus until the Feast of Pentecost, because there are great opportunities for work here, and I face many challenges.

If Timothy comes your way, make sure he feels safe among you, because he’s doing the Lord's work just like I am. Don't underestimate him; help him along his journey so he can join me. I’m looking forward to seeing him along with our other friends.

Stay alert, stand strong in your faith, be brave, and be strong! Make sure everything you do is rooted in love.

The churches in Asia send their greetings. Aquila and Prisca, along with the church that gathers in their home, send you their regards, as do all the brothers.

I, Paul, sign this greeting myself: 'May the love of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. My love is with you all in Christ Jesus.'

title: Paul'S Trials And Victories At Ephesus
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
After spending some time in Antioch, Paul set out on a journey through Galatia and Phrygia to strengthen the faith of all the disciples. He then returned to Ephesus. There, Paul went to the synagogue and spoke boldly for three months, arguing and trying to convince people about the Kingdom of God. But when some were stubborn and refused to listen, publicly slandering the Christian way, Paul took his disciples and left the synagogue. He then continued teaching daily in the lecture hall of Tyrannus. This went on for two years, so that everyone living in the province of Asia, both Jews and Greeks, heard the message of the Lord.

God performed amazing miracles through Paul, and the name of the Lord Jesus gained great respect. Many who believed confessed their wrongdoings and shared all the wicked things they had done.

During this time, a huge uproar arose over the Christian teachings. A silversmith named Demetrius, who made silver models of the temple of Artemis, called together his fellow workers and said, "Men, you know that this business brings us wealth. You also see and hear that not only in Ephesus but throughout all of Asia, this Paul has convinced many people by proclaiming that gods made by human hands aren’t real gods at all. There's a real danger that our trade will be affected, and that the temple of the great goddess Artemis will be neglected, diminishing her importance in all of Asia and the world."

When the workers heard this, they were furious and shouted, "Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!" The uproar spread throughout the city, and people rushed into the theater, dragging along Gaius and Aristarchus, Paul's companions from Macedonia. Paul wanted to join them, but the disciples wouldn't let him. Some of the leading religious officials in Asia, who were friends of Paul, sent warnings to him, urging him not to risk going into the theater.

People shouted all kinds of things; the assembly was in chaos, and most of those present didn’t even know why they were there. For about two hours, they yelled, "Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!" When the city clerk finally managed to quiet the crowd, he said: "Men of Ephesus, everyone knows that our city is the guardian of the temple of the great Artemis and the statue that fell from heaven. These facts can’t be denied, so you should stay calm and not act rashly. You’ve brought these men here who are neither robbers of temples nor blasphemers of our goddess. If Demetrius and his workers have a complaint against anyone, the courts are available, and the Roman officials can handle it; let both sides present their cases. If you want anything else, it must be sorted out in the regular assembly. We’re at risk of being charged with rioting today, as we have no clear reason for this gathering." With that, he dismissed the assembly.

When the chaos finally settled down, Paul called for the disciples and encouraged them. After saying goodbye, he set off for Macedonia.

title: Paul Writes To The Christians At Rome
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Paul, a servant of Jesus Christ and called to be an apostle, is set apart to share the good news about Jesus Christ our Lord with all of God's beloved people in Rome, who have been chosen to be His. Sending you love and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

First of all, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for every one of you because your faith has been reported all around the world. The God I serve with my spirit, as I share the good news about His Son, is my witness of how often I mention you in my prayers. I always ask that, if it is God's will, a way may be opened for me to visit you. I really want to see you so that I can share some spiritual gifts with you, so we can all be strengthened; more importantly, we can encourage each other through our faith—me by yours, and you by mine.

Dear friends, I want you to know that I've tried many times to come to you, but I've been prevented. I hope to gather some good results from my efforts among you as I have in other nations. I have a responsibility to everyone, both Greeks and non-Greeks, both the wise and the ignorant; that's why I'm eager to share the good news with you in Rome. I’m not ashamed of the good news because it is the power of God that can save everyone who believes, first for the Jew and then for the Greek.

Don’t follow the example of those who haven’t heard the good news. Instead, transform your mindset completely so that you can know what God’s will is—what is good, perfect, and pleasing to Him.

Let your love be genuine; hate what is evil and hold on to what is good. Have sincere devotion for one another as you love your fellow believers. Be eager to honor each other. Keep your passion alive; serve the Lord and rejoice in hope. Be patient during tough times, stay devoted to prayer, share with fellow Christians in need, and always be friendly and generous.

title: Paul Writes To The Christians At Rome
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Bless those who persecute you—bless and do not curse. Celebrate with those who are joyful, and grieve with those who weep. Be compassionate towards one another. Don’t set your sights too high; be ready to take on humble tasks. Avoid being arrogant.

Don’t retaliate evil for evil; strive to do what is honorable in everyone's eyes. If possible, as far as it depends on you, live peacefully with everyone. Never take revenge, dear friends, but let God handle those who wrong you. So if your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he's thirsty, give him a drink. By doing this, you'll pile up burning coals on his head. Don’t let evil win; instead, conquer evil with good.

Owe no one anything, except to love one another, because whoever loves their neighbor has fulfilled the law. All the commandments are summed up in this one command: "You shall love your neighbor as yourself." Love never harms a fellow human being; that’s why love fulfills all the law.

For several years, I've been eager to visit you on my way to Spain. I hope to see you as I head there, and then be sent on my journey by you after I’ve had the chance to enjoy your company for a while. But right now, I’m going to Jerusalem to serve God's people because the Christians in Macedonia and Greece have generously contributed to help the poor Christians in Jerusalem.

Now, I urge you, my brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love that His Spirit inspires, to join me in sincere prayer to God for me. Pray that I might be saved from those in Judea who reject Jesus, that my mission in Jerusalem will be well received by God's people, and that through God's will I can joyfully visit you and find some rest with you.

May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.

title: Paul'S Last Journey To Jerusalem
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
After saying goodbye to the elders in Ephesus, we sailed to Syria and docked at Tyre, where our ship needed to unload its cargo. There, we met some Christian disciples and spent a week with them. Guided by the Spirit, they urged Paul not to go to Jerusalem. But when it was time for us to leave, they all, including their wives and children, accompanied us as far as the city limits. We knelt on the beach, prayed, and said our goodbyes before boarding the ship while they returned home.

We continued our journey from Tyre to Ptolemais and completed our voyage. After greeting the Christian brothers who lived there, we spent a day with them. The following morning, we set off for Cæsarea, where we visited Philip the evangelist's house. He was one of the Seven and had four daughters who could prophesy.

While we were staying there for several days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea. He approached us, took Paul's belt, and tied up his own feet and hands with it, saying, "This is what the Holy Spirit says: 'The Jews will bind the owner of this belt in Jerusalem and hand him over to the Romans.'" When we and the local brothers heard this, we begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem. But he replied, "What do you mean by weeping and breaking my heart? I’m ready not just to be bound but even to die in Jerusalem for the Lord Jesus." When he couldn’t be convinced otherwise, we stopped pleading and said, "May the Lord's will be done."

A few days later, we set off for Jerusalem. Some disciples from Cæsarea joined us and took us to the home of Mnason from Cyprus, one of the early disciples, where we would stay. Upon reaching Jerusalem, the brothers welcomed us warmly.

The next day, Paul went with us to meet James, and all the elders of the church were there. After greeting them, Paul shared, one by one, all the things God had accomplished through his ministry among the foreign peoples. They praised God upon hearing it and said to him, "Brother, you can see how many thousands of Christian believers there are among the Jews, and they are all eager to uphold the law. They have been told that you teach all Jews in foreign lands not to follow the law of Moses. So what should we do? They will certainly hear that you've arrived; therefore, do this: we have four men here who have made a vow to offer certain sacrifices at the Temple. Join them, cover their expenses, and everyone will know that the rumors about you are false and that you live in accordance with the law of Moses."

So the next day, Paul joined the men and went with them into the Temple to announce the time when sacrifices would be offered for each of them.

title: Paul'S Narrow Escape From Death
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
As the seven days for the special offerings were coming to an end, some Jews from Asia spotted Paul in the Temple. They stirred up the crowd, shouting, "Men of Israel, help! This man is teaching everyone everywhere to disrespect our people, our law, and this sacred place!" The whole city was thrown into chaos. The crowd surged forward, grabbed Paul, and dragged him outside the Temple, slamming the doors shut behind them.

While the crowd was trying to kill Paul, word reached the commander of the soldiers that there was a major uproar in Jerusalem. He quickly gathered some soldiers and officers and rushed into the disorder. When the crowd saw him and the soldiers, they backed off from beating Paul. The commander approached, arrested Paul, and ordered him to be bound with two chains. "Who is he, and what has he done?" he inquired. Some in the crowd shouted one thing, others something else, and since the commander couldn't discover the truth amid the chaos, he commanded that Paul be taken to the castle. As they made their way to the steps, the soldiers had to carry Paul because of the crowd's violence, which followed them, yelling, "Kill him!"

Just as Paul was being taken into the castle, he turned to the commander and asked, "May I speak with you?" The commander replied, "Do you speak Greek? You're not the Egyptian who stirred up a rebellion and led four thousand outlaws into the desert, are you?" Paul replied, "I am a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of an important city. Please, let me address the people."

With permission from the commander, Paul stood on the steps, motioned for silence, and spoke to the crowd in Hebrew: "Brothers and fathers, please hear my defense!" The crowd quieted down even more when they heard him speaking in Hebrew. He continued, "I am a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia, but raised in this city. I studied under Gamaliel and was strict about our law. I was eager to serve God, just like you are today. I persecuted and even killed followers of Jesus, imprisoning both men and women, as the high priest and all the elders can confirm."

title: Paul'S Narrow Escape From Death
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
"I also had letters from them to our fellow Jews in Damascus, and I was on my way to bring the Christians there back to Jerusalem in chains for punishment. While on my journey, not far from Damascus, around noon, a bright light from heaven shone around me. I fell to the ground and heard a voice say, 'Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?' 'Who are you, Lord?' I asked. The voice answered, 'I am Jesus of Nazareth, the one you are persecuting.' I replied, 'What should I do, Lord?' And the Lord told me, 'Get up, go to Damascus, and there you will be told what to do.' Since I couldn't see because of the bright light, those traveling with me led me to Damascus. Then a man named Ananias, a religious individual respected by the Jews, came to me, stood beside me, and said, 'Brother Saul, regain your sight.' In that instant, I could see him. He then stated, 'The God of our ancestors has chosen you to know His will and see the Righteous One. You will be His witness to everyone of what you have seen and heard. Now go, for I will send you far away to non-Jews.'"

Until this point, the crowd had listened, but when they heard these words, they shouted, "Get rid of this man! He doesn’t deserve to live!" They threw off their coats and tossed dust into the air. The commander ordered that Paul be taken back to the castle and examined under flogging to find out why the crowd was against him. As they tied him up, Paul turned to the officer nearby and asked, "Is it legal for you to whip a Roman citizen without a trial?" When the officer heard this, he reported it to the commander, saying, "Be careful; this man is a Roman citizen." The commander approached Paul and asked, "Are you a Roman citizen?" Paul answered, "Yes." The commander replied, "I had to pay a lot for my citizenship!" Paul said, "But I was born a Roman citizen." The men assigned to examine Paul quickly stepped back from him, and the commander became frightened when he learned Paul was a Roman citizen.

title: Paul'S Narrow Escape From Death
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
The next day, the commander, wanting to find out the specific charges the Jews had against Paul, released him from his bonds and gathered the high priests and all the council members. They brought Paul down and placed him in front of them. Paul looked directly at the council members and declared, "Brothers, I have fulfilled my duty with a clear conscience before God until this very moment."

When Paul noticed that some on the council were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, "Brothers, I am a Pharisee, the son of Pharisees. It’s because of my hope in the resurrection of the dead that I’m standing trial!" This remark sparked a fierce argument between the Pharisees and Sadducees, leading to a significant clash of opinions as the Sadducees claim there is no resurrection, no angels, and no spirits, while the Pharisees believe in all of these. Some scribes from the Pharisees stood up and protested, "We find no fault in this man. What if an angel or spirit has spoken to him?" As the uproar grew louder, the commander feared for Paul's safety, so he ordered his troops to intervene and bring Paul back to the castle.

That night, the Lord stood next to Paul and said, "Take courage, for just as you have testified for me in Jerusalem, you must also do so in Rome."

Early the next morning, over forty Jews conspired together and made a vow not to eat or drink until they killed Paul. They approached the high priests and elders and said, "We have sworn not to taste any food until we have killed Paul. You and the council need to instruct the commander to bring Paul down to you, pretending you want to look more closely into the charges against him. We will be ready to ambush him before he arrives here."

But Paul’s nephew overheard their plot and went to the castle to inform Paul. Paul then called one of the officers and said, "Take this young man to the commander; he has something to share." The officer escorted him to the commander and said, "Paul, the prisoner, asked me to bring this young man to you because he has important information." The commander took the boy aside and asked him privately, "What do you need to tell me?" He replied, "The Jews have made an agreement to ask you to bring Paul down tomorrow to the council, pretending they want to inspect his case closely. Don’t agree to their request; over forty men are lying in wait to kill him and have sworn not to eat or drink until they do."

The commander let the young man leave, instructing him, "Don't tell anyone that you informed me of this." Then he called two officers and ordered, "Prepare two hundred soldiers, seventy cavalry, and two hundred spearmen by nine o'clock tonight to head to Caesarea." He also instructed them to provide horses for Paul to ride, ensuring his safe passage to Felix, the governor. The troops, as ordered, took Paul away by night to Antipatris. The next day, the soldiers returned to the castle, leaving the horsemen to continue with Paul. Upon reaching Caesarea, they presented Paul to the governor.

title: A Prisoner Who Preached To His Judges
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
A few days later, Felix arrived with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish. He summoned Paul to hear what he had to say about his faith in Christ Jesus. But when Paul spoke about living righteously, practicing self-control, and the impending judgment, Felix became uneasy and replied, "You can leave for now. I’ll call for you when it’s a better time." During all of this, Felix hoped that Paul would offer him money, which is why he frequently asked to meet with him. After two years, Felix was replaced by Porcius Festus, who, eager to win the favor of the Jews, opted to keep Paul imprisoned.

Three days after Festus became governor, he traveled from Cæsarea to Jerusalem. There, the high priests and important Jewish leaders presented their accusations against Paul and asked Festus to send for him to be brought to Jerusalem, intending to assassinate him on the way. Festus replied that Paul would stay in Cæsarea, where he was headed soon. "So," he said, "let your top leaders accompany me down and state whatever charges they have against him." After spending eight to ten days in Jerusalem, Festus returned to Cæsarea.

The next day, Festus took his place on the judgment seat and commanded that Paul be brought in. When Paul arrived, the Jewish leaders who had come down from Jerusalem surrounded him, accusing him with many serious claims that they couldn’t prove. In response, Paul stated, "I haven’t broken any Jewish laws or violated the Temple or the Emperor’s laws."

Festus, wanting to gain the Jews' favor, interrupted Paul and asked, "Are you willing to go to Jerusalem to be tried on these charges?" Paul answered, "I’m standing before the Emperor’s judgment seat, where I should be tried. You know very well I haven’t wronged the Jews. If I’ve broken the law or done anything deserving of death, I’m ready to accept that. But if their accusations are false, no one has the right to hand me over to them. I appeal to the Emperor!" After consulting with the council, Festus replied, "You have appealed to the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go."

title: A Prisoner Who Preached To His Judges
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk: After a few days, King Agrippa and Bernice came to Cæsarea to visit Festus. They stayed for quite a while, and Festus presented Paul’s case to the King. Agrippa expressed, "I’d like to hear this man for myself." "You’ll hear him tomorrow," Festus replied. The next day, Agrippa and Bernice arrived in the courtroom with great splendor, accompanied by commanders and leading citizens. At Festus's command, Paul was brought in, and Agrippa said to him, "You may defend yourself." Paul raised his hand and began his defense: "I’m grateful, King Agrippa, that I have this opportunity to defend myself against all the accusations from the Jews, as you are familiar with their customs and controversies. Please be patient as I share my story. Everyone knows the kind of life I lived among my own people from my youth, including in Jerusalem. As a Pharisee, I followed the strictest guidelines of our religion. I genuinely believed it was my responsibility to oppose the cause of Jesus of Nazareth, which I actively did in Jerusalem. With the high priests' authority, I imprisoned many of Jesus' followers and voted in favor of their executions. In every synagogue, I often punished them and tried to force them to speak against Jesus, and in my passionate rage, I even pursued them to distant cities.

title: A Prisoner Who Preached To His Judges
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
While traveling to Damascus for this mission, with written orders from the high priests, I saw a dazzling light from heaven around noon, brighter than the sun. We all fell to the ground, and I heard a voice saying to me in Hebrew, 'Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me? It’s hard for you to resist the truth.' I replied, 'Who are you, Lord?' and the Lord answered, 'I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting. Get up and stand on your feet, for I have chosen you to be my servant and a witness to what you’ve seen and what I will show you. I’ve sent you to the Jews and others to open their eyes, so they can turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, receiving forgiveness of their sins and a place among those who believe in me.' O King Agrippa, I have not disobeyed this heavenly vision. To this day, I have been supported by God, and I have been steadfast, sharing without changing a single word that the prophets and Moses foretold: that the Christ would suffer, rise from the dead, and bring the message of light not just to the Jews but to everyone."

After Paul finished his defense, Festus exclaimed, "Paul, you’re out of your mind! Your extensive knowledge has driven you insane!" But Paul replied, "I’m not crazy, most honorable Festus; I’m sharing the truth. The King knows about these matters, and I’m sure nothing has escaped his attention since they didn’t happen secretly. King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know you do." Agrippa responded to Paul, "You’re almost convincing me to become a Christian!" Paul answered, "I wish that whether soon or later, not only you but everyone who hears me today could be like me—except for these chains."

After that, the King, along with the governor, Bernice, and those sitting with them, stood up and, once they were alone, said to each other, "This man has done nothing that deserves death or imprisonment." And Agrippa told Festus, "He could have been released if he hadn’t appealed to the Emperor."

title: Paul'S Shipwreck
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
When it was decided that we would set sail for Italy, Paul and some other prisoners were placed under the care of Julius, an officer in the Emperor's regiment. We boarded a ship headed for the ports of Asia Minor. The next day, we stopped at Sidon, where Julius kindly allowed Paul to visit his friends and enjoy some hospitality. After that, we set sail again and moved along the southern coast of Cyprus, as the wind was against us. After passing Cilicia and Pamphylia, we arrived at Myra in Lycia. There, the officer found a ship from Alexandria heading for Italy and put us on board. We made slow progress for many days, and it took great effort to finally reach Cnidus. With the wind still opposing us, we sailed to the lee of Crete, opposite Cape Salmone, and after struggling along the coast, we reached a place called Fair Havens, near the city of Lasea.

Our journey had taken quite a while, and sailing had become more dangerous since it was late October. Paul warned the crew, saying, "Men, I see that this voyage will bring serious harm and loss, not just to the cargo and the ship but to our lives as well." However, the officer paid more attention to the captain and the shipowner than to Paul. Because the harbor wasn’t a good place to spend the winter, most of them advised setting sail again, hoping to reach Phœnix, which was a safer harbor for the winter.

When a light breeze from the south appeared, they believed they could reach Phœnix. So, after they lifted the anchor, they sailed close along the coast of Crete. But soon, a fierce wind called a "Northeaster" struck us. The ship was caught in it and couldn’t head into the wind, so we had no choice but to let the storm carry us. We ran under the protection of a small island called Cauda, where we struggled to bring in the ship's boat. After securing it on board, the crew used ropes to tie the ship together. Fearing we might run aground on the quicksands of Africa, they lowered the sails and let the ship drift. As the storm raged on, the men began throwing the cargo overboard. On the third day, they even tossed the ship's tackle over the side. With neither sun nor stars visible for many days, and the gale continuing, all hope of being saved faded.

title: Paul'S Shipwreck
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
After a long time without food, Paul stood up among the crew and said, "Men, you should have listened to me and not left Crete. You could have avoided this hardship and loss. But now I urge you to keep your spirits up, for no one will lose their life, but we will lose the ship. Last night, an angel of the God I serve came to me and said, 'Paul, don’t be afraid; you must stand before the Emperor. God has also granted you the lives of everyone sailing with you.' So cheer up! I believe God, and I’m certain it will happen just as I was told; but we will be shipwrecked on an island."

On the fourteenth night of drifting in the Adriatic Sea, the sailors noticed they might be close to land around midnight. They took soundings and measured one hundred and twenty feet of water; after going a bit further, they measured ninety feet. Fearing they would crash into the rocks, they dropped four anchors from the stern and prayed for daylight. The sailors wanted to escape the ship and lower the lifeboat into the sea, pretending to lay out anchors from the bow, but Paul told the officer and the soldiers, "Unless these men stay on board, we can’t be saved." So the soldiers cut the ropes holding the lifeboat and let it drift away.

As dawn approached, Paul urged everyone to eat, saying, "You’ve been on edge for fourteen days, taking little to no food. I urge you to eat something because this will keep you alive, and not one of you will lose even a single hair from your head." After saying this, he took some bread, gave thanks to God in front of everyone, broke the bread, and began to eat it. This brought everyone some cheer, and they began to eat as well. There were about seventy-six of us on board. After eating enough, they lightened the ship by throwing the wheat into the sea.

When day broke, they couldn’t tell what land it was, but they saw an inlet with a sandy beach where they hoped to run the ship ashore. They cut away the anchors and left them in the sea. At the same time, they unfastened the ropes from the rudders and hoisted the foresail to the wind, aiming for the beach. However, when they reached a place where two seas met, the ship ran aground. The bow stuck fast and couldn’t be moved, while the stern began to break apart amid the crashing waves. The soldiers wanted to kill the prisoners, fearing some might swim to shore and escape. But since the officer wanted to save Paul, he stopped them and ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first, while the rest followed using planks and other debris from the ship. Everyone made it safely to shore. After we escaped, we found the island was called Malta.

title: The End Of Paul'S Long Journey
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
The islanders greeted us with incredible warmth. They built a fire and welcomed us, especially since it was pouring rain and quite chilly. While Paul was gathering sticks and adding them to the fire, a poisonous snake, disturbed by the heat, latched onto his hand. The natives saw the snake hanging from his hand and whispered to each other, 'This man must be a murderer. Even though he survived the sea, justice won’t let him live.' But Paul shook the snake off into the fire and suffered no harm. They expected him to swell up or collapse instantly, but after waiting a long time and seeing no harm come to him, they changed their minds and started saying he was a god.

On the part of the island where we landed, there was an estate owned by Publius, the governor. He welcomed us warmly and treated us generously for three days. Unfortunately, his father was ill with fever and dysentery. Paul went to see him, prayed, and placed his hands on him, healing him. After that, other sick people from the island came to Paul and were cured too. They showered us with gifts, and when we set sail, they provided us with everything we needed.

After three months, we boarded a ship from Alexandria called 'The Twin Brothers,' which had wintered at the island. We stopped at Syracuse and stayed there for three days. Then we sailed on to Rhegium. The next day, a southern wind arose, and we arrived the following day in Puteoli, where we met Christian brothers who invited us to stay with them for a week, before continuing on our journey to Rome.

When the brothers in Rome heard about us, they traveled as far as the Market of Appius and the Three Taverns to greet us. When Paul saw them, he was thankful to God and felt encouraged.

title: The End Of Paul'S Long Journey
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
When we finally arrived in Rome, Paul was allowed to live in his own place, under the guard of a soldier. Three days after we got there, Paul invited the leading Jewish leaders to meet with him. He said, 'Brothers, I’ve done nothing against our Jewish laws or traditions, but I was handed over as a prisoner from Jerusalem to the Romans. They examined me and wanted to release me because I was innocent of any crime that deserved death. But the Jews protested, so I had to appeal to the Emperor—not that I have anything against my own people. That’s why I wanted to meet with you and share what’s on my mind; I am in chains because of the hope of Israel.'

They replied, 'We haven't received any letters from Judea about you, and no one has come here with any negative reports about you. We want to hear from you what you believe, because we know that the Christian faith is under attack everywhere.' They scheduled a day for more of them to come and meet with him. From morning to evening, Paul explained his teachings, talked about the Kingdom of God, and tried to persuade them to believe in Jesus, using evidence from the law of Moses and the prophets. Some accepted what he said, while others refused to believe. When they couldn’t reach an agreement, they left after Paul told them, 'The Holy Spirit was right when he spoke to your ancestors through the prophet Isaiah:'

"'Go to this people and say to them,
You will hear but never understand,
You will look but never see;
For this people's hearts are hard,
And their ears can't hear,
And they have shut their eyes,
So they won't see with their eyes,
Or hear with their ears,
Or understand with their hearts,
And turn so I can heal them.'"

"So remember, this chance for salvation that God has given you is also offered to other people, and they will accept it."

Paul spent two whole years in his own rented house. He welcomed anyone who came to see him, sharing the message of the Kingdom of God and teaching about the Lord Jesus Christ openly, without anyone stopping him.

title: Paul'S Last Words To His Friends
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Paul and Timothy, servants of Jesus Christ, send greetings to all the followers of Christ in Philippi, including the ministers and their helpers. May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ grant you love and peace.

If the Lord allows, I hope to send Timothy to you soon, as I would love to hear your news. There’s no one like him who truly cares for your well-being; most people are only looking out for themselves, not for the interests of Christ Jesus. You know how Timothy has proven himself, working alongside me like a son with his father in spreading the good news. I intend to send him as soon as I know how things will turn out for me; I’m confident that if the Lord permits, I will also come to you soon.

Always rejoice in the Lord. I’ll say it again—rejoice! Let everyone know you are gentle and patient. Don’t be anxious; instead, always share your requests with God through sincere prayer and thanksgiving. Then, the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and minds in Christ Jesus.

Finally, my friends, think about whatever is true, honorable, just, pure, lovely, and admirable. If there's anything virtuous or deserving of praise, focus on those things. Practice what you’ve learned and received from me, and the God of peace will be with you.

As a Christian brother, it brings me great joy to hear that you are thinking of me again. You have always cared, even if you didn’t have the chance to show it before. I don’t mention this because I’m in need; I’ve learned to be content in every situation. I know how to live simply and how to thrive in abundance. I've discovered the secret to being content, whether I have plenty or am hungry, whether I’m in abundance or in need. I can do everything through Christ who strengthens me.

You acted honorably by supporting me during my troubles. Even when I was in Thessalonica, you sent help more than once. I’m not just after the gift itself, but I appreciate how it adds to your account! I have more than enough; I’m fully supplied by what I received from you through Epaphroditus. It’s like a sweet-smelling sacrifice, pleasing and acceptable to God. My God will meet all your needs according to His glorious wealth in Christ Jesus. Glory be to God our Father forever and ever.

My time is almost up, and I can feel that I’m nearing the end. I have fought the good fight, finished the race, and kept my faith. Now, the crown of righteousness is waiting for me, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will give to me on that day, and not just to me but to all who have longed for His appearing.

title: The Meaning Of Faith
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Faith is being sure that we will get what we hope for; it’s proof of things we can't see. It was through their faith that the heroes of the past were celebrated by God. Through faith, we understand that the universe was created perfectly by God's command, and everything we see was made from what was not visible.

By faith, Abel offered a better sacrifice than Cain and received the recognition of being a good man because God accepted his gifts. Though he is gone, his faith still speaks to us.

Through faith, Noah was warned by God about things that hadn’t happened yet, so he built an ark to save his family out of deep respect for that command. In doing this, he condemned the world and became an heir to the righteousness that comes from faith.

Because of faith, Abraham obeyed when he was called to go to a place he would inherit, setting out without knowing where he was headed. He made his home in the promised land like it was a foreign place, living in tents with Isaac and Jacob, who shared this promise with him.

When tested, Abraham showed his faith by being ready to sacrifice Isaac, his only son, even after God had promised that his family name would continue through Isaac. He believed God could bring people back to life, and in a way, he did receive his son back from death.

By faith, Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau and shared glimpses of the future with them. Joseph, while dying, thought about the time when the Israelites would leave Egypt and instructed them about his bones.

Moses was hidden by his parents for three months after his birth because they recognized how special he was, and they didn’t fear the King’s order.

When Moses grew up, he turned down the identity of Pharaoh's daughter’s son. He chose to endure hardship with God’s people rather than enjoy the fleeting pleasures of wrongdoing.

He left Egypt not out of fear of the King’s anger, but as someone who could see the invisible King, never wavering in his determination.

Through faith, the Israelites walked through the Red Sea as if it were dry land, while the Egyptians who tried to cross were drowned.

What else can I say? There isn't enough time to share about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets—they conquered kingdoms through faith, did what was right, received God’s promises, silenced lions, extinguished flames, escaped the sword, found strength in weakness, and triumphed in battle.

Women resumed their lives, receiving their loved ones back from the dead. Others faced torture, refusing to give in so they could rise to a better life. Some endured criticism and suffering, even chains and imprisonment. They were stoned, burned, sawed in half, and slain by the sword. They wandered in sheepskin and goatskin, lacking basic needs, being mistreated—these were the people the world was unworthy of, roaming the wild, the hills, caves, and hiding in the ground. They all earned God’s approval through faith, but they didn’t receive the promise, for God had a greater plan so that they wouldn’t be made perfect without us.

So, since we’re surrounded by a great crowd of witnesses, let’s throw off everything that hinders us and the sin that clings tightly. Let’s run with patience the race that’s ahead of us, keeping our eyes focused on Jesus, the Pioneer and Perfecter of faith. For the joy that was set before him, he endured the cross, disregarding its shame, and now he’s seated at the right hand of God’s throne.

title: The Importance Of Doing What Is Right
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, greets the true Israelites scattered across the nations.

My friends, consider it an opportunity for joy when you face all sorts of challenges. Understand that testing your faith produces patience; let your patience complete its work so you can be whole and lacking nothing.

If any of you need wisdom, ask God, who gives generously to everyone without holding back, and it will be given to you. Just make sure you ask with faith, never doubting, because the person who doubts is like the waves of the sea, tossed around by the wind. Don’t let such a person think they will receive anything from God, since a half-hearted individual is unstable in all their ways.

Blessed is the person who withstands temptation; for when they have passed the test, they will receive the crown of life promised to all who love God. No one should say they are tempted by God, because God doesn’t tempt anyone to do wrong. Each person is tempted when their own evil desire pulls them away. That desire gives birth to sin, and when sin grows up, it leads to death.

Don’t be misled, my dear friends. Every good gift and every perfect blessing comes from above, from the Father who is the source of all light, and there is no change or shadow in Him.

Know this, dear friends: everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to become angry, because human anger doesn’t lead to the righteousness God desires. So set aside all that is harmful and wicked, and humbly accept the truth that has taken root in you, which can save your souls.

Follow the commands of that truth, and don’t just listen and fool yourselves. If someone hears that truth but doesn’t act on it, they are like a person who looks in a mirror, sees their reflection, walks away, and immediately forgets what they look like. But the one who gazes into the perfect law of freedom and continues to do so—not just listening and forgetting, but putting in the effort—will be blessed in what they do.

If anyone thinks they are religious but can’t control their tongue, they are deceiving themselves; their religion is worthless. True religion, which is pure and faultless in the eyes of God our Father, is to care for orphans and widows in their distress and to keep oneself untainted by the world.

title: The Love That Makes Men Brothers
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
We understand love through this: Christ gave his life for us, so we should also be willing to give our lives for each other. But if someone has the resources of this world and watches a brother struggle without offering help, how can we say that God's love lives in that person? My dear friends, let’s express our love not just in words or empty promises, but through our actions and genuine care.

Dear friends, let’s love one another, because love originates from God. Everyone who loves is a child of God and knows Him. If someone doesn’t love others, they don’t truly know God, because God is love. God demonstrated His love for us by sending His only Son into the world, so that through Him we might find true life. His love is visible not because we loved Him first, but because He loved us and sent His Son as a sacrifice to forgive our sins.

Friends, if God loves us this way, we should love one another as well. No one has ever seen God; but if we love each other, then God lives in us, and His love is completed within us. We know we live in Him and He in us, because He has shared a piece of His Spirit with us. We have witnessed and can testify that the Father sent the Son to save the world.

We know and believe in the love that God has for us. God is love, and anyone whose life is filled with love lives in God, and God lives in them. Love has no fear; in fact, perfect love drives out fear, because fear involves punishment, and whoever fears has not yet reached perfection in love. We love Him because He loved us first.

If someone says, "I love God," but hates their brother, they are lying; because anyone who does not love their brother, whom they can see, cannot truly love God, whom they cannot see. And we have this command from Him: anyone who loves God must also love their brother.

Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ is a child of God, and everyone who loves the Father also loves His children. We know we are His children when we love Him and obey His commands, because loving God means following His commands. And His commands aren’t burdensome, for everyone born of God conquers the world. Our faith is the victory that conquers the world. Who truly conquers the world, except for those who believe that Jesus is the Son of God?

The confidence we have in God is this: He listens to us whenever we ask for anything that aligns with His will. And since we know He hears whatever we ask, we can be sure that we have received what we’ve asked of Him.

title: The Glory And Honor That Jesus Has Won
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
John writes to the seven churches in the province of Asia. I hope you receive blessings and peace from the one who is, who was, and who always will be; from the seven spirits standing before his throne; and from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness, the first to be raised from the dead and the ruler of the kings on earth.

I, John, your brother, share in the struggles, the authority, and the patience that comes from our faith in Jesus. I found myself on the island of Patmos because of my commitment to God's message and my testimony about Jesus. On the Lord's Day, I was in the Spirit and heard a loud voice behind me, like a trumpet, calling out, "Write down what you see in a book and send it to the seven churches."

I turned to see who was speaking to me, and as I turned, I saw seven golden lamps. In the middle of the lamps was someone who looked like a Son of Man, wearing a long robe with a gold belt around his chest. His hair was white like wool, as white as snow; his eyes were like flames of fire, his feet like polished bronze glowing in a furnace, and his voice was like the roar of rushing water. In his right hand, he held seven stars, and a sharp two-edged sword came from his mouth. His face shone like the sun at its brightest.

When I saw him, I fell at his feet, feeling as if I were dead. But he placed his hand on me and said, "Don’t be afraid; I am the First and the Last. I was dead, but now I am alive forever. Write down what you see, what is now, and what will happen later. The seven stars you saw in my right hand represent the angels of the seven churches, and the seven lamps stand for the seven churches."

title: The Glory And Honor That Jesus Has Won
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
After this, I saw a door open in heaven. The voice, like a trumpet, that I had heard earlier said, "Come up here, and I will show you what must happen next." At once, I was caught up in the Spirit, and I saw a throne in heaven, looking like a precious jewel. Someone was sitting on the throne. Surrounding the throne was a rainbow that looked like emerald; around the throne were twenty-four other thrones, and seated on them were twenty-four elders dressed in white robes and wearing golden crowns. From the throne came lightning, voices, and thunder, while seven flaming torches in front of the throne represented the seven spirits of God.

In front of the throne, there was what looked like a sea of glass, clear as crystal. Surrounding the throne were four living creatures, and they never stopped day or night chanting.

"Holy, holy, holy is the Lord God Almighty, who was, is, and always will be."

Then I noticed a book sealed with seven seals, lying at the right hand of the one seated on the throne. A mighty angel cried out loudly, "Who is worthy to open the book and break its seals?" But no one in heaven or on earth or under the earth was found worthy to open the book or even look into it. I began to weep bitterly because no one was found worthy. Then one of the elders said to me, "Stop crying! Look, the Lion from the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has conquered! He is worthy to open the book and its seven seals."

Then, between the throne and the four living creatures, I saw a Lamb standing as though it had been slain. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God sent out into all the earth. The Lamb approached and took the book from the right hand of the one on the throne. When he took the book, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb. Each had a harp and golden bowls filled with incense, representing the prayers of the saints. They sang a new song: "You are worthy to take the book and open its seals because you were slain. By your blood, you ransomed people for God from every tribe, language, people, and nation; you made them a kingdom and priests to our God, and they will reign on earth."

Then I looked and heard the voices of countless angels around the throne, along with the living creatures and the elders. Their number was in the millions, crying out loudly, "Worthy is the Lamb who was slain to receive power, wealth, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and blessing!" I heard every creature in heaven, on earth, under the earth, and in the sea praising, "To the one seated on the throne and to the Lamb be blessing, honor, glory, and power forever and ever!" The four living creatures said, "Amen," and the elders fell down and worshipped.

title: The Reward Of The Faithful
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
Then I saw an enormous crowd that no one could count, made up of people from every nation, tribe, and language. They stood before the throne and the Lamb, wearing white robes and holding palm branches. They shouted, "Salvation belongs to our God who sits on the throne and to the Lamb!"

One of the elders turned to me and asked, "Who are these people in white robes, and where did they come from?" I replied, "You know, sir." He then told me, "These are the ones who have gone through great suffering and have washed their robes, making them white in the blood of the Lamb. Because of this, they are now standing before God's throne and serve Him day and night in His temple. The one sitting on the throne will protect them; they will never again go hungry or thirsty; the sun or any scorching heat will not harm them. The Lamb, who stands before the throne, will be their shepherd, leading them to streams of living water, and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes."

Next, I saw another angel flying high in the sky, bringing an everlasting message of good news for everyone on earth, for every nation, tribe, language, and people. He shouted, "Fear God and give Him glory, for the hour of His judgment has come. Worship Him who made the heavens, the earth, the sea, and the springs of water."

I saw a magnificent white throne, and someone was sitting on it. From His presence, the earth and sky vanished and could no longer be found. I saw the dead, both great and small, standing before the throne. Then books were opened, and another book was opened—the Book of Life. The dead were judged based on what was recorded in the books, according to their actions. The sea gave up its dead, and Death and the Underworld surrendered their dead as well, and everyone was judged according to their deeds.

title: The New Heaven On Earth
author: The Children's Bible
text_chunk:
I saw a brand new heaven and a new earth, because the old heaven and the old earth had disappeared, and the sea was gone. Then I saw the Holy City, the New Jerusalem, coming down from God in heaven, all dressed up like a bride ready to meet her husband. I heard a loud voice from the throne say, "Look! God’s home is now among people. He will live with them, and they will be his people. God himself will be with them. He will wipe away every tear from their eyes, and there will be no more death, sorrow, wailing, or pain, because all those things are gone." The One sitting on the throne said, "Look, I am making everything new!" He also added, "Write this down: 'These words are trustworthy and true.'"

He then said to me, "Everything has come to an end! I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. I will give water from the fountain of life to anyone who is thirsty. Those who overcome will inherit this, and I will be their God, and they will be my children."

The city won’t need the sun or the moon for light because the glory of God will light it up, and its light will be the Lamb. The gates will never be closed during the day, and there won’t be any night. Nothing impure or anyone who does shameful or dishonest things will enter it; only those whose names are written in the Lamb’s book of life will be allowed in.

Then he showed me the river of the water of life, clear as crystal, flowing through the streets of the city from the throne of God and the Lamb. On both sides of the river, there was the tree of life, which produced twelve kinds of fruit and yielded its fruit every month. The leaves of the tree were for healing the nations.

In that city, the throne of God and the Lamb will be present, and his servants will serve and worship him. They will see his face, and his name will be written on their foreheads. There will be no more night, and they won’t need lamps or sunlight because the Lord God will be their light, and they will reign forever.

He told me, "Don’t hide the prophetic words in this book, because the time for their fulfillment is near. Let the one who does wrong keep doing wrong, and the one who is filthy keep being filthy. Let the one who is righteous keep doing right, and the one who is pure keep being pure. Know this: I am coming soon, and I will bring my rewards to repay everyone based on what they have done. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. Blessed are those who wash their robes, so they may have the right to the tree of life and enter through the gates into the city."

"I, Jesus, have sent my angel to share this message with you for the churches. I am the descendant of David, the bright Morning Star. The Spirit and the Bride say, 'Come.' Let anyone who hears say, 'Come.' Let whoever is thirsty come, and whoever wishes can take the water of life freely."

"For God loved the world so much that he gave his only Son, so that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have everlasting life."
